This file is a merged representation of a subset of the codebase, containing files not matching ignore patterns, combined into a single document by Repomix.

<file_summary>
This section contains a summary of this file.

<purpose>
This file contains a packed representation of a subset of the repository's contents that is considered the most important context.
It is designed to be easily consumable by AI systems for analysis, code review,
or other automated processes.
</purpose>

<file_format>
The content is organized as follows:
1. This summary section
2. Repository information
3. Directory structure
4. Repository files (if enabled)
5. Multiple file entries, each consisting of:
  - File path as an attribute
  - Full contents of the file
</file_format>

<usage_guidelines>
- This file should be treated as read-only. Any changes should be made to the
  original repository files, not this packed version.
- When processing this file, use the file path to distinguish
  between different files in the repository.
- Be aware that this file may contain sensitive information. Handle it with
  the same level of security as you would the original repository.
</usage_guidelines>

<notes>
- Some files may have been excluded based on .gitignore rules and Repomix's configuration
- Binary files are not included in this packed representation. Please refer to the Repository Structure section for a complete list of file paths, including binary files
- Files matching these patterns are excluded: node_modules, docs/node_modules, .beads, *.lock, *.log
- Files matching patterns in .gitignore are excluded
- Files matching default ignore patterns are excluded
- Files are sorted by Git change count (files with more changes are at the bottom)
</notes>

</file_summary>

<file path=".docs/beads/templates/epic-bugfix.yaml">
# Beads Task Template: Bug Fix
# Use: bd create --file .docs/beads/templates/epic-bugfix.yaml

template: epic-bugfix
title: "[Bug] {issue_title}"
type: bug
priority: 1  # P1 = high

description: |
  ## Problem
  {describe_the_issue}

  ## Expected Behavior
  {what_should_happen}

  ## Actual Behavior
  {what_actually_happens}

  ## Steps to Reproduce
  1. {step_1}
  2. {step_2}
  3. {step_3}

  ## Environment
  - Service: {service_name}
  - Namespace: {namespace}
  - Chart version: {version}

  ## Investigation Notes
  {any_findings_so_far}

  ## Root Cause (to be filled)
  TBD

  ## Fix Approach (to be filled)
  TBD

subtasks:
  - title: "Investigate {issue_title}"
    type: task
    agent: devops-troubleshooter
    description: |
      Debug commands:
      ```bash
      kubectl logs -n {namespace} -l app.kubernetes.io/name={service} --tail=100
      kubectl describe pod -n {namespace} <pod>
      kubectl exec -n {namespace} <pod> -- nc -zv postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local 5432
      ```

  - title: "Fix {issue_title}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-developer
    description: |
      Apply fix following patterns in .docs/arch-rules.md.
      Update tests if behavior changed.

  - title: "Verify fix for {issue_title}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-tester
    description: |
      - Deploy fixed version
      - Verify issue resolved
      - Run regression tests
      - Confirm no side effects
</file>

<file path=".docs/beads/templates/epic-feature.yaml">
# Beads Task Template: New Feature
# Use: bd create --file .docs/beads/templates/epic-feature.yaml

template: epic-feature
title: "[Feature] {feature_name}"
type: epic
priority: 2  # P2 = medium

description: |
  ## Overview
  {brief_description}

  ## Business Requirements
  Reference: .docs/requirements.md section {section_ref}

  ## Technical Approach
  Reference: .docs/arch-rules.md pattern {pattern_ref}

  ## Acceptance Criteria
  - [ ] {criterion_1}
  - [ ] {criterion_2}
  - [ ] {criterion_3}

  ## Dependencies
  - {dependency_if_any}

  ## Affected Components
  - kubernetes/charts/{service}/
  - kubernetes/releases/{service}.yaml.gotmpl
  - .docs/architecture.md (update after completion)

  ## Quality Gates
  - [ ] helm lint passes
  - [ ] kubeval validates
  - [ ] NetworkPolicy configured
  - [ ] ServiceMonitor enabled
  - [ ] Health checks defined
  - [ ] .docs/architecture.md updated

subtasks:
  - title: "Design {feature_name} architecture"
    type: task
    agent: spec-architect
    description: |
      Review .docs/arch-rules.md and design the implementation.
      Update .docs/architecture.md with proposed changes.

  - title: "Implement {feature_name}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-developer
    description: |
      Implement following patterns in .docs/arch-rules.md.
      Ensure all mandatory templates exist.

  - title: "Test {feature_name}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-tester
    description: |
      - Run helm lint
      - Run kubeval
      - Deploy and verify pod status
      - Run Helm tests

  - title: "Review {feature_name}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-reviewer
    description: |
      Security review:
      - NetworkPolicy rules
      - RBAC permissions
      - Security context
      - No hardcoded secrets
</file>

<file path=".docs/beads/templates/epic-new-service.yaml">
# Beads Task Template: New Service Deployment
# Use: bd create --file .docs/beads/templates/epic-new-service.yaml

template: epic-new-service
title: "[Service] Deploy {service_name}"
type: epic
priority: 2  # P2 = medium

description: |
  ## Overview
  Deploy {service_name} to the self-hosted platform.

  ## Service Information
  - **Name**: {service_name}
  - **Namespace**: {namespace}
  - **Type**: {web-app|api|worker|database}
  - **Image**: {image_repository}:{version}
  - **Documentation**: {upstream_docs_url}

  ## Requirements
  Reference: .docs/requirements.md section {section_ref}

  ### Dependencies
  - [ ] PostgreSQL database
  - [ ] MongoDB database
  - [ ] Valkey cache
  - [ ] MinIO storage
  - [ ] Authentik SSO
  - [ ] Vault secrets

  ### Resources
  - CPU: {cpu_request} / {cpu_limit}
  - Memory: {memory_request} / {memory_limit}
  - Storage: {storage_size}

  ## Implementation Checklist
  Based on .docs/arch-rules.md:

  ### Helm Chart Creation
  - [ ] Create kubernetes/charts/{service}/
  - [ ] Chart.yaml with metadata
  - [ ] values.yaml with defaults
  - [ ] README.md

  ### Mandatory Templates
  - [ ] _helpers.tpl
  - [ ] serviceaccount.yaml
  - [ ] rbac.yaml (least privilege)
  - [ ] networkpolicy.yaml (explicit rules)
  - [ ] deployment.yaml (security context, probes)
  - [ ] service.yaml
  - [ ] ingress.yaml (Authentik middleware)
  - [ ] servicemonitor.yaml
  - [ ] pdb.yaml
  - [ ] tests/test-connection.yaml

  ### Configuration
  - [ ] Database connection to .db namespace
  - [ ] Vault annotations for secrets
  - [ ] Health checks (liveness, readiness, startup)
  - [ ] Resource requests/limits

  ### Registration
  - [ ] Add to kubernetes/apps/_others.yaml
  - [ ] Create kubernetes/releases/{service}.yaml.gotmpl

  ### Validation
  - [ ] helm lint passes
  - [ ] kubeval validates
  - [ ] Deploy successful
  - [ ] Pod Running
  - [ ] Helm test passes
  - [ ] .docs/architecture.md updated

subtasks:
  - title: "Research {service_name} requirements"
    type: task
    agent: spec-analyst
    description: |
      Review upstream documentation for:
      - Required environment variables
      - Database requirements
      - Storage requirements
      - Health check endpoints
      - Metrics endpoint

  - title: "Create Helm chart for {service_name}"
    type: task
    agent: spec-developer
    description: |
      1. Copy similar chart from kubernetes/charts/
      2. Customize for {service_name}
      3. Follow .docs/arch-rules.md HELM-* patterns
      4. Ensure all mandatory templates

  - title: "Configure {service_name} deployment"
    type: task
    agent: spec-developer
    description: |
      1. Add to kubernetes/apps/_others.yaml
      2. Create releases/{service}.yaml.gotmpl
      3. Configure database connections
      4. Add Vault secret annotations

  - title: "Test {service_name} deployment"
    type: task
    agent: spec-tester
    description: |
      1. helm lint kubernetes/charts/{service}
      2. helmfile -e k8s diff --selector name={service}
      3. Deploy and verify
      4. Run Helm tests

  - title: "Review {service_name} security"
    type: task
    agent: spec-reviewer
    description: |
      Verify:
      - NetworkPolicy rules correct
      - RBAC minimal permissions
      - Security context applied
      - No hardcoded secrets
      - Database using .db namespace

  - title: "Update documentation for {service_name}"
    type: task
    agent: technical-writer
    description: |
      Update .docs/architecture.md:
      - Add to Component Inventory
      - Document dependencies
      - Add to resource allocation
</file>

<file path=".docs/context/ansible.md">
# Ansible Agent Context

**Module**: Ansible
**Path**: `ansible/`
**Purpose**: Infrastructure provisioning, configuration, and deployment orchestration

---

## Architecture Overview

Ansible is the **only tool for server provisioning and configuration**. All deployments run through:

```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.ini all.yml --vault-password-file ~/.ansible_vault_password
```

The `infrastructure` role calls Helmfile with appropriate selectors for Kubernetes deployments.

```
CLI → Ansible → Helmfile → Kubernetes
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
ansible/
├── all.yml                        # Main playbook entry point
├── inventory/
│   ├── hosts.ini                  # Main inventory (gitignored)
│   ├── hosts.example.ini          # Template
│   ├── master.ini                 # Control plane only
│   ├── gateway.ini                # Gateway VPS only
│   └── node.ini                   # Worker nodes only
├── group_vars/
│   └── all/
│       ├── vault.yml              # ENCRYPTED secrets
│       └── vault.example.yml      # Template
├── roles/
│   ├── docker/                    # Docker installation
│   ├── security/                  # Firewall, hardening
│   ├── kubespray/                 # Kubernetes deployment
│   ├── infrastructure/            # Helmfile execution
│   ├── pangolin/                  # VPN setup
│   ├── validate/                  # Verification tests
│   └── ...
└── .credentials/                  # Generated credentials (gitignored)
```

---

## Key Tags

| Tag | Description | Roles |
|-----|-------------|-------|
| `server` | Base server preparation | docker, security |
| `docker` | Docker runtime | docker |
| `kubespray` | Kubernetes cluster | kubespray |
| `kubernetes` | K8s configuration | kubespray |
| `storage` | Storage provisioner | storage, openebs |
| `openebs` | OpenEBS operators | openebs |
| `infrastructure` | All Helmfile services | infrastructure |
| `base` | Core services only | infrastructure (subset) |
| `databases` | Database services | infrastructure (subset) |
| `apps` | Application services | infrastructure (subset) |
| `pangolin` | VPN configuration | pangolin |
| `validate` | Verification tests | validate |

---

## Inventory Format

```ini
[all:vars]
ansible_user=admin
ansible_python_interpreter=/usr/bin/python3
ansible_ssh_private_key_file=~/.ssh/id_ed25519

[master]
local-server ansible_host=192.168.100.2 node_role=master

[workers]
# worker01 ansible_host=192.168.100.3 node_role=worker

[gateway]
gateway01 ansible_host=80.90.178.207 ansible_user=root node_role=gateway

[k8s:children]
master
workers

[infrastructure:children]
k8s
gateway
```

---

## Vault Variables

All secrets are stored in `group_vars/all/vault.yml` (encrypted).

**Structure**:
```yaml
user:
  name: 'admin'
  password: '$6$...'       # mkpasswd --method=sha-512
  ssh_keys:
    - 'ssh-ed25519 ...'
  email: 'admin@example.com'

databases:
  postgresql:
    admin_password: '...'
    replication_password: '...'
  mongodb:
    admin_password: '...'
  valkey:
    password: '...'
  minio:
    root_user: 'minio-admin'
    root_password: '...'

oauth:
  authentik:
    bootstrap_password: '...'
    secret_key: '...'
  gitlab:
    root_password: '...'

monitoring:
  grafana:
    admin_password: '...'

vpn:
  pangolin:
    # WireGuard keys
```

**Commands**:
```bash
# Encrypt
ansible-vault encrypt group_vars/all/vault.yml

# Edit
ansible-vault edit group_vars/all/vault.yml --vault-password-file ~/.ansible_vault_password

# View
ansible-vault view group_vars/all/vault.yml --vault-password-file ~/.ansible_vault_password
```

---

## Infrastructure Role

The `infrastructure` role (`roles/infrastructure/`) is the bridge to Helmfile:

```yaml
# roles/infrastructure/tasks/main.yml
- name: Generate Helmfile inventory values
  template:
    src: inventory-values.yaml.j2
    dest: "{{ kubernetes_path }}/envs/k8s/inventory-values.yaml"

- name: Run Helmfile apply
  command: >
    helmfile -e k8s apply
    {% if helmfile_selector is defined %}--selector {{ helmfile_selector }}{% endif %}
  args:
    chdir: "{{ kubernetes_path }}"
  environment:
    KUBECONFIG: "{{ kubeconfig_path }}"
```

**Tag-based execution**:
- `--tags infrastructure,base` → Deploys core services
- `--tags infrastructure,databases` → Deploys databases
- `--tags infrastructure,apps` → Deploys applications

---

## Pangolin VPN Role

Sets up WireGuard tunnel between gateway VPS and cluster:

**Gateway** (`roles/pangolin/tasks/gateway.yml`):
- Installs WireGuard
- Configures Pangolin server
- Sets up Traefik edge router
- Enables IP forwarding and NAT

**Cluster** (`roles/pangolin/tasks/cluster.yml`):
- Deploys Pangolin client in Kubernetes
- Establishes VPN tunnel to gateway
- Registers routes with gateway Traefik

---

## Development Guidelines

### Adding a New Role

1. Create `ansible/roles/<name>/`
2. Structure:
   ```
   roles/<name>/
   ├── defaults/main.yml      # Default variables
   ├── tasks/main.yml         # Main tasks
   ├── handlers/main.yml      # Event handlers
   ├── templates/             # Jinja2 templates
   ├── files/                 # Static files
   └── meta/main.yml          # Dependencies
   ```
3. Add tag to `all.yml`

### Task Best Practices

```yaml
- name: Descriptive task name
  module:
    param: value
  become: true                 # When root needed
  when: condition              # Conditional execution
  notify: Handler name         # Trigger handler
  tags: [tag1, tag2]          # For selective execution
  register: result             # Capture output
  changed_when: false          # Mark idempotent
```

### Handler Pattern

```yaml
# handlers/main.yml
- name: Restart service
  systemd:
    name: myservice
    state: restarted
```

---

## Testing

```bash
# Syntax check
ansible-playbook --syntax-check all.yml

# Dry run (check mode)
ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.ini all.yml --check

# Lint
ansible-lint *.yml

# Run specific tags
ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.ini all.yml --tags validate
```

---

## Common Issues

### "Vault password required"
Ensure `~/.ansible_vault_password` exists with the decryption password.

### "SSH connection failed"
Check SSH key path in inventory and that the key is loaded (`ssh-add`).

### "No hosts matched"
Verify inventory file path and host group names.

---

## Integration Points

| System | Integration |
|--------|-------------|
| CLI | Spawned by `deploy.command.ts` |
| Helmfile | Called by `infrastructure` role |
| Vault (Ansible) | Encrypts `group_vars/all/vault.yml` |
| Kubernetes | Kubeconfig path configured in role |
</file>

<file path=".docs/context/cli.md">
# CLI Agent Context

**Module**: CLI (selfhost)
**Path**: `cli/`
**Framework**: NestJS with Commander.js
**Purpose**: User interface for deployment orchestration and monitoring

---

## Architecture Overview

The CLI is a **wrapper around Ansible** that provides:
- Interactive deployment prompts
- Phase-based deployment management
- Monitoring daemon with Telegram alerts
- Service selection and configuration

```
User → CLI (selfhost deploy) → Ansible (all.yml) → Helmfile → Kubernetes
```

**The CLI does NOT directly interact with Kubernetes or Helm.**

---

## Module Structure

```
cli/
├── src/
│   ├── app.module.ts              # Root module
│   ├── commands/                  # CLI commands
│   │   ├── deploy.command.ts      # Main deployment command
│   │   ├── services.command.ts    # Service management
│   │   ├── inventory.command.ts   # Host inventory
│   │   ├── monitor.command.ts     # TUI monitoring
│   │   ├── bot.command.ts         # Telegram bot
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── modules/
│   │   ├── services/              # Service configuration
│   │   ├── inventory/             # Host inventory management
│   │   ├── config/                # CLI configuration
│   │   ├── monitor/               # Monitoring TUI
│   │   ├── ui/                    # Prompts and UI helpers
│   │   └── balancing/             # Load balancing strategies
│   ├── telegram/                  # Telegram bot handlers
│   └── daemon/                    # Background daemon service
```

---

## Key Commands

### `selfhost deploy`

Main deployment command that orchestrates Ansible execution.

```bash
selfhost deploy                    # Full interactive deployment
selfhost deploy --tags databases   # Run specific Ansible tags
selfhost deploy --dry-run          # Preview without changes
selfhost deploy --resume           # Resume incomplete deployment
selfhost deploy --bypass-permissions  # Skip confirmations
```

**Phase-to-Tags mapping** (from `deploy.command.ts`):

| Phase | Ansible Tags |
|-------|--------------|
| INFRASTRUCTURE_SETUP | server, docker |
| KUBERNETES_BOOTSTRAP | kubespray, kubernetes |
| STORAGE_LAYER | storage, openebs |
| BACKUP_SETUP | backup, backup-node, zerobyte |
| CORE_SERVICES | infrastructure, base |
| DATABASES | infrastructure, databases |
| APPLICATION_SERVICES | infrastructure, apps |
| NETWORK_GATEWAY | pangolin |
| VERIFICATION | validate, verify |

### `selfhost services`

Manage service selection for deployment.

```bash
selfhost services list             # Show all services
selfhost services enable <name>    # Enable service
selfhost services disable <name>   # Disable service
```

### `selfhost inventory`

Manage host inventory.

```bash
selfhost inventory list            # Show hosts
selfhost inventory add             # Add new host
selfhost inventory validate        # Validate configuration
```

### `selfhost monitor`

TUI monitoring interface.

```bash
selfhost monitor                   # Launch TUI
selfhost monitor start             # Start daemon
selfhost monitor stop              # Stop daemon
```

---

## Service Selection

Services are defined in `kubernetes/apps/_others.yaml` and parsed by `ServicesService`.

**Service tiers**:
- **Tier 0 (Core)**: Always deployed, non-selectable
- **Tier 1 (Infrastructure)**: Recommended, selectable
- **Tier 2 (Databases)**: Selectable based on app needs
- **Tier 3 (Applications)**: Fully user-selectable

The CLI presents a multi-select prompt for non-core services.

---

## Configuration Storage

CLI stores state in `~/.selfhost/`:
- `config.json` — CLI configuration
- `deployment-state.json` — Active deployment tracking
- `services.json` — Enabled services cache

---

## Development Guidelines

### Adding a New Command

1. Create `cli/src/commands/<name>.command.ts`
2. Export a `create<Name>Command(app: INestApplicationContext)` function
3. Register in `cli/src/main.ts`

### Working with Services

```typescript
// Get service by name
const service = servicesService.getByName('gitlab');

// Get enabled services
const enabled = servicesService.getEnabled();

// Enable/disable
servicesService.setEnabled('gitlab', true);
```

### Ansible Execution

```typescript
import { executeAnsible } from './commands/deploy.command';

const result = await executeAnsible(
  ansiblePath,
  ['infrastructure', 'databases'],  // tags
  'hosts.ini',                       // inventory file
  false                              // dryRun
);
```

---

## Testing

```bash
cd cli
npm run test              # Unit tests
npm run test:e2e          # E2E tests
npm run lint              # Lint check
```

---

## Common Issues

### "CLI not initialized"
Run `selfhost init` first to create configuration.

### "Could not find repository root"
Ensure you're running from within the self-hosted directory.

### "Inventory validation failed"
Check that `ansible/inventory/hosts.ini` exists and is properly formatted.

---

## Integration Points

| System | Integration |
|--------|-------------|
| Ansible | Spawns `ansible-playbook` subprocess |
| Kubernetes | Reads service definitions from `apps/_others.yaml` |
| Telegram | Bot handlers in `telegram/handlers/` |
| Prometheus | API client in `modules/monitor/apis/` |
</file>

<file path=".docs/context/kubernetes.md">
# Kubernetes Agent Context

**Module**: Kubernetes (Helm/Helmfile)
**Path**: `kubernetes/`
**Purpose**: Service deployment and management via Helm charts

---

## Architecture Overview

Helmfile is the **single entry point for all Helm chart deployments**:

```bash
helmfile -e k8s apply --selector name=<service>
```

**Never call Helmfile directly in production** - use Ansible infrastructure role.

---

## Directory Structure

```
kubernetes/
├── helmfile.yaml                  # Entry point
├── .helmfile/
│   ├── environments.yaml.gotmpl   # Environment configuration
│   ├── repositories.yaml          # Helm repositories
│   └── releases.yaml.gotmpl       # Release template
├── apps/
│   └── _others.yaml               # Service registry
├── charts/
│   └── <service>/                 # Custom Helm charts
│       ├── Chart.yaml
│       ├── values.yaml
│       ├── README.md
│       └── templates/
│           ├── _helpers.tpl
│           ├── serviceaccount.yaml
│           ├── rbac.yaml
│           ├── networkpolicy.yaml
│           ├── deployment.yaml
│           ├── service.yaml
│           ├── ingress.yaml
│           ├── servicemonitor.yaml
│           ├── pdb.yaml
│           └── tests/
│               └── test-connection.yaml
├── releases/
│   └── <service>.yaml.gotmpl      # Release value overrides
└── envs/
    └── k8s/
        ├── values.yaml            # Environment values
        └── secrets/
            └── _all.yaml          # SOPS-encrypted secrets
```

---

## Service Registry

Services are defined in `apps/_others.yaml`:

```yaml
gitlab:
  repo: charts                     # or external repo name
  chart: gitlab                    # chart name
  namespace: code                  # target namespace
  version: v1.0.0                  # chart version
  installed: true                  # false to disable
  needs:
    - ingress/traefik
    - service/vault
    - db/postgresql                # database dependency
```

**Deployment order is enforced by `needs`**:
```
namespaces → traefik → consul → vault → cert-manager → authentik → databases → applications
```

---

## Namespace Layout

| Namespace | Purpose | Services |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| `ingress` | Edge routing | Traefik, Consul |
| `service` | Platform services | Vault, Authentik, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, cert-manager |
| `db` | Data layer | PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Valkey, MinIO, ClickHouse, MySQL, RabbitMQ |
| `code` | Development | GitLab, TeamCity, YouTrack, Hub, Coder |
| `productivity` | Collaboration | Affine, Excalidraw, Penpot, Notesnook |
| `social` | Communication | Stoat (Revolt), Stalwart (mail) |
| `data` | Personal data | Vaultwarden, Syncthing, Nextcloud, Rybbit |
| `infrastructure` | Operations | Glance, Bytebase, Pangolin, Harbor |
| `automation` | Workflows | Kestra, N8n |
| `content` | Publishing | Ghost |
| `utilities` | Miscellaneous | Vert, Metube |

---

## Database Connections

**All databases are in `db` namespace.** Use FQDN:

```yaml
# CORRECT
postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local:5432
mongodb.db.svc.cluster.local:27017
valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local:6379
minio.db.svc.cluster.local:9000
clickhouse.db.svc.cluster.local:8123
mysql.db.svc.cluster.local:3306
rabbitmq.db.svc.cluster.local:5672

# WRONG
postgresql.code.svc.cluster.local  # Wrong namespace!
postgresql                          # Missing namespace!
```

---

## Mandatory Chart Templates

Every custom chart MUST include:

| Template | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `_helpers.tpl` | Template functions (fullname, labels, etc.) |
| `serviceaccount.yaml` | Pod identity |
| `rbac.yaml` | Role + RoleBinding (least privilege) |
| `networkpolicy.yaml` | Explicit ingress/egress rules |
| `deployment.yaml` | With security context, probes, resources |
| `service.yaml` | ClusterIP service |
| `ingress.yaml` | With Traefik and Authentik middleware |
| `servicemonitor.yaml` | Prometheus scraping |
| `pdb.yaml` | PodDisruptionBudget |
| `tests/test-connection.yaml` | Helm test |

---

## Security Context (Required)

```yaml
# values.yaml
podSecurityContext:
  runAsNonRoot: true
  runAsUser: 1000
  fsGroup: 1000
  seccompProfile:
    type: RuntimeDefault

securityContext:
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  capabilities:
    drop:
      - ALL
```

Use `emptyDir` volumes for writable paths (`/tmp`, `/cache`).

---

## Vault Integration

**Never create Kubernetes Secrets for credentials.** Use Vault Agent Injector:

```yaml
# deployment.yaml annotations
vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject: "true"
vault.hashicorp.com/role: "<service>-role"
vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject-secret-database: "database/creds/<service>-role"
vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject-template-database: |
  {{- with secret "database/creds/<service>-role" -}}
  export DB_USERNAME="{{ .Data.username }}"
  export DB_PASSWORD="{{ .Data.password }}"
  {{- end }}
```

---

## NetworkPolicy Pattern

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: {{ include "chart.fullname" . }}
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "chart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
    - Egress
  ingress:
    # Allow from Traefik
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: ingress
          podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app.kubernetes.io/name: traefik
      ports:
        - port: {{ .Values.service.targetPort }}
    # Allow Prometheus scraping
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: service
          podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app.kubernetes.io/name: prometheus
      ports:
        - port: metrics
  egress:
    # Allow DNS
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: kube-system
      ports:
        - port: 53
          protocol: UDP
    # Allow database access
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: db
      ports:
        - port: 5432  # PostgreSQL
```

---

## Helmfile Commands

```bash
# Preview changes
helmfile -e k8s diff --selector name=<service>

# Deploy single service
helmfile -e k8s apply --selector name=<service>

# Deploy all
helmfile -e k8s apply

# Destroy
helmfile -e k8s destroy --selector name=<service>

# Sync (update existing)
helmfile -e k8s sync --selector name=<service>
```

---

## Value Precedence

1. Chart defaults (`charts/<service>/values.yaml`) — lowest
2. Release overrides (`releases/<service>.yaml.gotmpl`)
3. Environment values (`envs/k8s/values.yaml`)
4. SOPS secrets (`envs/k8s/secrets/_all.yaml`) — highest

---

## Development Guidelines

### Creating New Chart

1. Copy from similar chart in `kubernetes/charts/`
2. Update `Chart.yaml` with name, version, description
3. Customize `values.yaml` for the service
4. Ensure all mandatory templates exist
5. Add NetworkPolicy rules for dependencies
6. Register in `apps/_others.yaml`
7. Create `releases/<service>.yaml.gotmpl`
8. Validate: `helm lint kubernetes/charts/<service>`

### Validation Commands

```bash
# Lint chart
helm lint kubernetes/charts/<service>

# Template and validate
helm template kubernetes/charts/<service> | kubeval

# Diff before apply
helmfile -e k8s diff --selector name=<service>
```

---

## Common Issues

### Pod CrashLoopBackOff

```bash
# Check logs
kubectl logs -n <namespace> <pod> --previous

# Common fixes:
# 1. Database connection - verify FQDN uses .db namespace
# 2. Missing secrets - check Vault annotations
# 3. Health check - increase initialDelaySeconds
# 4. Permission denied - add emptyDir for writable paths
```

### NetworkPolicy Blocking Traffic

```bash
# Debug
kubectl describe networkpolicy <name> -n <namespace>
kubectl exec -n <namespace> <pod> -- nc -zv postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local 5432

# Fix: Add egress rule for database namespace
```

### Prometheus Not Scraping

```bash
# Check ServiceMonitor exists
kubectl get servicemonitor -n <namespace>

# Fix: Ensure label is present
metadata:
  labels:
    prometheus: kube-prometheus
```

---

## SOPS Secrets

```bash
# Edit encrypted secrets
sops kubernetes/envs/k8s/secrets/_all.yaml

# View decrypted
sops -d kubernetes/envs/k8s/secrets/_all.yaml
```

---

## Integration Points

| System | Integration |
|--------|-------------|
| Ansible | Called by `infrastructure` role |
| Vault | Secrets injected via Agent Injector |
| Traefik | Ingress with Authentik middleware |
| Prometheus | ServiceMonitor CRD |
| cert-manager | TLS certificates |
</file>

<file path=".docs/Constitution.md">
# Project Constitution

**Self-Hosted Infrastructure Platform**
**Version:** 1.0.0
**Effective Date:** February 2026

---

## Core Principles

### 1. Security First
- **Zero Trust**: All network traffic requires explicit NetworkPolicy rules
- **Secrets in Vault**: Never create Kubernetes Secrets for credentials; use Vault Agent Injector
- **Least Privilege**: RBAC roles grant only minimum required permissions
- **Non-root Containers**: All pods run as non-root user (UID 1000)
- **Read-only Filesystem**: Containers use read-only root filesystem where possible

### 2. Infrastructure as Code
- **Everything Declarative**: All infrastructure defined in version-controlled code
- **No Manual Changes**: Direct `kubectl apply` or manual changes are prohibited
- **Idempotent Operations**: Running playbooks multiple times produces the same result
- **Single Command Deploy**: Full stack deploys via single Ansible run

### 3. Resource Efficiency
- **Database Consolidation**: Services share PostgreSQL/MongoDB/Valkey clusters
- **Connection Pooling**: PgBouncer for PostgreSQL, proper pool sizing
- **Resource Limits**: All pods have defined requests and limits
- **Storage Optimization**: Use dynamic provisioning, request only needed capacity

### 4. Observability from Day One
- **100% Metrics Coverage**: Every service exposes `/metrics` endpoint
- **Structured Logging**: JSON format to stdout/stderr, collected by Loki
- **ServiceMonitor Required**: All charts include Prometheus ServiceMonitor
- **Alerting**: PrometheusRules for critical failure scenarios

---

## Architecture Rules

### Domain Responsibility Separation

```
CLI (selfhost) --> Ansible (all.yml) --> Helmfile --> Kubernetes
     │                  │                    │
     │                  │                    └── Chart deployment
     │                  └── Server provisioning, orchestration
     └── User interface, monitoring daemon
```

**NEVER:**
- Call `helmfile apply` directly in production
- Create Kubernetes resources with `kubectl apply`
- Store secrets in plain text anywhere
- Bypass Ansible for server configuration

### Database Connection Strings

All databases live in the `db` namespace. Use FQDN:

```yaml
# CORRECT
postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local:5432
mongodb.db.svc.cluster.local:27017
valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local:6379

# WRONG
postgresql.code.svc.cluster.local  # Wrong namespace!
postgresql                          # Missing namespace!
```

### Helm Chart Requirements

Every custom chart MUST include:

| Template | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `serviceaccount.yaml` | Pod identity |
| `rbac.yaml` | Role + RoleBinding |
| `networkpolicy.yaml` | Explicit ingress/egress |
| `servicemonitor.yaml` | Prometheus scraping |
| `deployment.yaml` | With security context |
| `pdb.yaml` | PodDisruptionBudget |
| `tests/test-connection.yaml` | Helm test |

---

## Code Standards

### Formatting & Linting

| Tool | Target | Command |
|------|--------|---------|
| helm lint | Helm charts | `helm lint kubernetes/charts/<service>` |
| kubeval | K8s manifests | `helm template <chart> \| kubeval` |
| ansible-lint | Playbooks | `cd ansible && ansible-lint *.yml` |
| ESLint | TypeScript (CLI) | `npm run lint` |

### Naming Conventions

```yaml
Helm Charts:
  directory: lowercase-hyphenated (e.g., "my-service")
  templates: lowercase.yaml (e.g., "deployment.yaml")
  values: values.yaml

Kubernetes:
  labels: app.kubernetes.io/* standard labels
  namespaces: lowercase (ingress, service, db, code, etc.)

Ansible:
  playbooks: lowercase_underscored.yml
  roles: lowercase_underscored
  variables: snake_case
  tags: lowercase
```

### Image Policy

- **Always pin exact versions** (e.g., `v1.2.3`)
- **Never use `:latest`**
- **Prefer SHA digests** for production

---

## Agent Guidelines

### For spec-developer

1. **Read first**: Check `.docs/arch-rules.md` for templates
2. **Copy existing**: Use similar chart as starting point
3. **Validate always**: Run `helm lint` before committing
4. **Update docs**: Modify `.docs/architecture.md` after changes

### For spec-reviewer

1. **Security check**: Verify NetworkPolicy, RBAC, SecurityContext
2. **Resource check**: Confirm requests/limits are defined
3. **Secret check**: No hardcoded credentials
4. **Namespace check**: Database connections use `.db.svc.cluster.local`

### For spec-tester

1. **Helm tests**: Verify `tests/test-connection.yaml` exists
2. **Dry run**: Use `helmfile diff` before apply
3. **Pod status**: Confirm pods reach Running state
4. **Metrics**: Verify ServiceMonitor scrapes successfully

### For team-lead

1. **Orchestrate**: Spawn appropriate specialist agents
2. **Track progress**: Use Beads for task management
3. **Quality gates**: Enforce all validation before merging
4. **Documentation**: Ensure `.docs/architecture.md` is updated

---

## Deployment Phases

| Phase | Ansible Tags | Description |
|-------|--------------|-------------|
| 0 | `server`, `docker` | Infrastructure setup |
| 1 | `kubespray` | Kubernetes bootstrap |
| 2 | `storage`, `openebs` | Storage layer |
| 3 | `backup`, `zerobyte` | Backup setup |
| 4 | `infrastructure`, `base` | Core services |
| 5 | `infrastructure`, `databases` | Databases |
| 6 | `infrastructure`, `apps` | Applications |
| 7 | `pangolin` | Network gateway |
| 8 | `validate` | Verification |

---

## Quality Checklist

Before merging any change:

- [ ] `helm lint` passes without errors
- [ ] `kubeval` validates all manifests
- [ ] No secrets in plain text (use Vault)
- [ ] ServiceAccount + RBAC present
- [ ] NetworkPolicy with explicit rules
- [ ] Resource requests/limits defined
- [ ] ServiceMonitor configured
- [ ] Health checks (liveness + readiness + startup)
- [ ] `.docs/architecture.md` updated

---

## Emergency Procedures

### Rollback Deployment

```bash
# Rollback specific service
helm rollback <service> -n <namespace>

# Via Helmfile
helmfile -e k8s -l name=<service> destroy
helmfile -e k8s -l name=<service> apply --set image.tag=<previous-version>
```

### Debug Pod Issues

```bash
# Check logs
kubectl logs -n <namespace> <pod> --previous

# Describe pod
kubectl describe pod -n <namespace> <pod>

# Network connectivity
kubectl exec -n <namespace> <pod> -- nc -zv postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local 5432
```

---

**This constitution is binding for all AI agents and human contributors.**
</file>

<file path=".docs/project.yaml">
version: "2.0"
project:
  name: "self-hosted"
  description: "Enterprise-grade self-hosted infrastructure platform with 42+ services on Kubernetes"
  type: "monorepo"
  license: "MIT"

tech_stack:
  primary_languages:
    - TypeScript  # CLI
    - YAML        # Kubernetes, Ansible, Helmfile
    - Jinja2      # Ansible templates, Helm templates
  secondary_languages:
    - Bash        # Scripts
    - Go          # Helm templating
  frameworks:
    - NestJS      # CLI framework
    - Helm        # Kubernetes package manager
    - Ansible     # Infrastructure automation
    - Helmfile    # Helm deployment orchestration
  runtime: "node"
  package_manager: "npm"
  databases:
    - PostgreSQL  # Primary relational (shared instance)
    - MongoDB     # Document store
    - Valkey      # Redis-compatible cache
    - MinIO       # S3-compatible object storage
    - ClickHouse  # OLAP analytics
    - MySQL       # Ghost CMS
    - RabbitMQ    # Message queue

architecture:
  style: "microservices"
  patterns:
    - "Infrastructure as Code"
    - "GitOps"
    - "Zero Trust Networking"
    - "Secrets Management (Vault)"
    - "Service Mesh (Consul)"

# Domain responsibility separation
domains:
  - name: "CLI"
    description: "User interface for deployment orchestration"
    path: "cli/"
    entities: ["DeployCommand", "ServicesService", "InventoryService", "MonitorService"]
    bounded_context: "deployment-orchestration"

  - name: "Ansible"
    description: "Infrastructure provisioning and configuration"
    path: "ansible/"
    entities: ["Playbooks", "Roles", "Inventory", "Vault"]
    bounded_context: "infrastructure-provisioning"

  - name: "Kubernetes"
    description: "Service deployment and management"
    path: "kubernetes/"
    entities: ["HelmCharts", "Releases", "Helmfile", "Namespaces"]
    bounded_context: "service-deployment"

# Service tiers for deployment ordering
service_tiers:
  tier_0_core:
    description: "Foundational infrastructure - always deployed"
    services:
      - namespaces
      - traefik
      - consul
      - vault
      - cert-manager
      - pangolin
      - authentik
    user_selectable: false

  tier_1_infrastructure:
    description: "Observability and tooling"
    services:
      - monitoring
      - logging
      - glance
      - openebs
    user_selectable: true
    recommended: true

  tier_2_databases:
    description: "Data persistence layer"
    services:
      - postgresql
      - mongodb
      - valkey
      - minio
      - clickhouse
      - mysql
      - rabbitmq
    user_selectable: true

  tier_3_applications:
    description: "User-facing services"
    namespaces:
      code: [gitlab, hub, youtrack, teamcity, coder]
      productivity: [affine, notesnook, excalidraw, penpot]
      social: [stoat, stalwart]
      data: [vaultwarden, syncthing, nextcloud, rybbit]
      automation: [kestra, n8n]
      content: [ghost]
      utilities: [vert, metube]
      infrastructure: [bytebase, harbor, remnawave]
    user_selectable: true

# Namespace organization
namespaces:
  ingress: "Edge routing and load balancing"
  service: "Platform services (Vault, Authentik, Monitoring)"
  db: "Data layer (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Valkey, MinIO, ClickHouse)"
  code: "Development tools (GitLab, CI/CD)"
  productivity: "Collaboration tools"
  social: "Communication services"
  data: "Personal data management"
  infrastructure: "Operations tools"
  automation: "Workflow automation"
  content: "Publishing"
  utilities: "Miscellaneous tools"

# Tooling configuration
tooling:
  package_manager: "npm"
  beads:
    enabled: true
    workspace: ".beads"
  repomix:
    enabled: true
  validation:
    helm_lint: true
    kubeval: true
    ansible_lint: true
    secret_scan: true

# Quality gates
quality_gates:
  planning:
    threshold: 95
    validators:
      - requirements_coverage
      - architecture_completeness
  development:
    threshold: 85
    validators:
      - helm_lint
      - kubeval
      - ansible_lint
      - security_scan
  validation:
    threshold: 95
    validators:
      - all_tests_pass
      - documentation_complete
      - architecture_updated

# Agent preferences
agent_preferences:
  code_style: "concise"
  review_strictness: "strict"
  auto_fix: true
  documentation_required: true
  always_update_architecture: true

# Critical paths
critical_paths:
  documentation:
    - ".docs/requirements.md"     # WHAT to build
    - ".docs/arch-rules.md"       # HOW to build
    - ".docs/architecture.md"     # Current state (update after changes)
  deployment:
    - "ansible/all.yml"           # Main playbook
    - "kubernetes/helmfile.yaml"  # Helmfile entry point
    - "kubernetes/apps/_others.yaml"  # Service registry
  cli:
    - "cli/src/commands/deploy.command.ts"
    - "cli/src/modules/services/services.service.ts"
</file>

<file path=".docs/quality-gates.yaml">
# Quality Gates Configuration
# Defines validation thresholds and validators for each development phase

version: "1.0"

gates:
  # Planning phase - before implementation begins
  planning:
    threshold: 95
    description: "Requirements and architecture must be fully understood"
    validators:
      - id: requirements_coverage
        description: "All requirements from .docs/requirements.md understood"
        weight: 30
      - id: architecture_review
        description: "Architecture patterns from .docs/arch-rules.md reviewed"
        weight: 30
      - id: current_state_check
        description: "Current state in .docs/architecture.md analyzed"
        weight: 20
      - id: dependencies_identified
        description: "Service dependencies mapped"
        weight: 20

  # Development phase - during implementation
  development:
    threshold: 85
    description: "Code quality and security standards"
    validators:
      - id: helm_lint
        description: "helm lint passes without errors"
        command: "helm lint kubernetes/charts/{service}"
        weight: 20
      - id: kubeval
        description: "Kubernetes manifests validate"
        command: "helm template kubernetes/charts/{service} | kubeval"
        weight: 15
      - id: ansible_lint
        description: "Ansible playbooks pass linting"
        command: "cd ansible && ansible-lint *.yml"
        weight: 15
        condition: "ansible_changed"
      - id: security_context
        description: "Pods run as non-root with read-only filesystem"
        weight: 15
      - id: network_policy
        description: "NetworkPolicy with explicit rules exists"
        weight: 15
      - id: rbac
        description: "ServiceAccount and RBAC configured"
        weight: 10
      - id: secret_scan
        description: "No hardcoded secrets"
        command: "git diff | grep -Ei 'password|secret|token|api[_-]?key'"
        expect_empty: true
        weight: 10

  # Validation phase - before merge/deploy
  validation:
    threshold: 95
    description: "Full validation before deployment"
    validators:
      - id: all_tests_pass
        description: "Helm tests and integration tests pass"
        weight: 25
      - id: deployment_successful
        description: "Service deploys without errors"
        weight: 20
      - id: pod_running
        description: "Pod reaches Running state"
        weight: 15
      - id: health_checks
        description: "Liveness, readiness, startup probes configured"
        weight: 10
      - id: servicemonitor
        description: "Prometheus ServiceMonitor configured"
        weight: 10
      - id: documentation_complete
        description: ".docs/architecture.md updated"
        weight: 20

# Mandatory templates for Helm charts
helm_chart_requirements:
  templates:
    required:
      - "_helpers.tpl"
      - "serviceaccount.yaml"
      - "rbac.yaml"
      - "networkpolicy.yaml"
      - "deployment.yaml"
      - "service.yaml"
      - "servicemonitor.yaml"
      - "pdb.yaml"
      - "tests/test-connection.yaml"
    optional:
      - "ingress.yaml"
      - "configmap.yaml"
      - "pvc.yaml"
      - "hpa.yaml"
      - "prometheusrule.yaml"

  values_structure:
    required:
      - "image.repository"
      - "image.tag"
      - "replicaCount"
      - "resources.requests"
      - "resources.limits"
      - "serviceAccount.create"
      - "podSecurityContext.runAsNonRoot"
      - "securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation"
      - "networkPolicy.enabled"
      - "monitoring.enabled"

# Security requirements
security:
  pod_security_context:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 1000
    fsGroup: 1000
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault

  container_security_context:
    allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
    readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    capabilities:
      drop:
        - ALL

  prohibited:
    - "image.tag: latest"
    - "securityContext.privileged: true"
    - "hostNetwork: true"
    - "hostPID: true"
    - "kubernetes Secret with credentials"

# Database connection patterns
database_patterns:
  correct:
    postgresql: "postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local:5432"
    mongodb: "mongodb.db.svc.cluster.local:27017"
    valkey: "valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local:6379"
    minio: "minio.db.svc.cluster.local:9000"
    clickhouse: "clickhouse.db.svc.cluster.local:8123"
    mysql: "mysql.db.svc.cluster.local:3306"
    rabbitmq: "rabbitmq.db.svc.cluster.local:5672"

  prohibited_patterns:
    - "*.code.svc.cluster.local"      # Wrong namespace
    - "*.productivity.svc.cluster.local"
    - "postgresql"                     # Missing namespace

# Validation commands
commands:
  pre_commit:
    - name: "Helm lint"
      command: "helm lint kubernetes/charts/{service}"
    - name: "Kubeval"
      command: "helm template kubernetes/charts/{service} | kubeval"
    - name: "Secret scan"
      command: "git diff | grep -Ei 'password|secret|token|api[_-]?key'"
      expect_empty: true

  pre_deploy:
    - name: "Helmfile diff"
      command: "helmfile -e k8s diff --selector name={service}"
    - name: "Validate inventory"
      command: "ansible-playbook --syntax-check ansible/all.yml"

  post_deploy:
    - name: "Check pod status"
      command: "kubectl get pods -n {namespace} -l app.kubernetes.io/name={service}"
    - name: "Run Helm tests"
      command: "helm test {service} -n {namespace}"
    - name: "Verify metrics"
      command: "kubectl get servicemonitor -n {namespace} {service}"
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/infrastructure/tasks/generate_helmfile_values.yml">
---
# Generate Helmfile inventory values from Ansible inventory
# Creates kubernetes/envs/k8s/values/inventory-values.yaml with dynamic IPs

- name: Display inventory value generation plan
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Generating Helmfile values from Ansible inventory"
      - "Gateway: {{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_host'] | default('not defined') }}"
      - "Masters: {{ groups['masters'] | length }}"
      - "Workers: {{ groups['workers'] | length }}"
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]

- name: Ensure values directory exists
  file:
    path: "{{ kubernetes_path }}/envs/k8s/values"
    state: directory
    mode: '0755'
  delegate_to: localhost
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]

- name: Generate inventory-values.yaml
  template:
    src: inventory-values.yaml.j2
    dest: "{{ kubernetes_path }}/envs/k8s/values/inventory-values.yaml"
    mode: '0644'
  delegate_to: localhost
  register: inventory_values_generated
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]

- name: Display generated values path
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Generated: {{ kubernetes_path }}/envs/k8s/values/inventory-values.yaml"
      - "Changed: {{ inventory_values_generated.changed }}"
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]

- name: Verify generated values file
  stat:
    path: "{{ kubernetes_path }}/envs/k8s/values/inventory-values.yaml"
  delegate_to: localhost
  register: values_file_stat
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]

- name: Display values file status
  debug:
    msg: "Values file exists: {{ values_file_stat.stat.exists }}, size: {{ values_file_stat.stat.size | default(0) }} bytes"
  tags: [infrastructure, generate-values]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/infrastructure/tasks/observability.yml">
---
# Observability stack deployment
# Monitoring (Prometheus + Grafana) and Logging (Loki + Promtail)
# Can be run independently with: --tags infrastructure,observability

- name: Display observability deployment plan
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Deploying observability stack"
      - "Services: Monitoring (Prometheus + Grafana), Logging (Loki + Promtail)"
  tags: [infrastructure, observability]

# ============================================================
# MONITORING (Prometheus + Grafana + Alertmanager)
# ============================================================

- name: Deploy Monitoring stack
  command: >
    helmfile -e {{ helmfile_environment }}
    apply
    --selector name=monitoring
    --suppress-secrets
  args:
    chdir: "{{ kubernetes_path }}"
  environment:
    KUBECONFIG: "{{ kubeconfig_path }}"
  register: monitoring_deploy
  changed_when: "'has been upgraded' in monitoring_deploy.stdout or 'installed' in monitoring_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Wait for Prometheus pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
    --timeout=300s
  register: prometheus_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Wait for Grafana pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana
    --timeout=180s
  register: grafana_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Wait for Alertmanager pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
    --timeout=180s
  register: alertmanager_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Get Prometheus service endpoint
  command: kubectl get svc -n service prometheus-server -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}:{.spec.ports[0].port}'
  register: prometheus_endpoint
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Get Grafana service endpoint
  command: kubectl get svc -n service grafana -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}:{.spec.ports[0].port}'
  register: grafana_endpoint
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

- name: Display monitoring status
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Monitoring stack deployment:"
      - "  Prometheus: {{ prometheus_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Grafana: {{ grafana_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Alertmanager: {{ alertmanager_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "Endpoints:"
      - "  Prometheus: {{ prometheus_endpoint.stdout | default('N/A') }}"
      - "  Grafana: {{ grafana_endpoint.stdout | default('N/A') }}"
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, monitoring]

# ============================================================
# LOGGING (Loki + Promtail)
# ============================================================

- name: Deploy Logging stack
  command: >
    helmfile -e {{ helmfile_environment }}
    apply
    --selector name=logging
    --suppress-secrets
  args:
    chdir: "{{ kubernetes_path }}"
  environment:
    KUBECONFIG: "{{ kubeconfig_path }}"
  register: logging_deploy
  changed_when: "'has been upgraded' in logging_deploy.stdout or 'installed' in logging_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, logging]

- name: Wait for Loki pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=loki
    --timeout=300s
  register: loki_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, logging]

- name: Wait for Promtail pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=promtail
    --timeout=180s
  register: promtail_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, logging]

- name: Get Loki service endpoint
  command: kubectl get svc -n service loki -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}:{.spec.ports[0].port}'
  register: loki_endpoint
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, logging]

- name: Display logging status
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Logging stack deployment:"
      - "  Loki: {{ loki_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Promtail: {{ promtail_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "Endpoints:"
      - "  Loki: {{ loki_endpoint.stdout | default('N/A') }}"
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, logging]

# ============================================================
# VERIFICATION
# ============================================================

- name: Verify Prometheus can scrape targets
  command: >
    kubectl exec -n service deploy/prometheus-server --
    wget -qO- http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets | head -100
  register: prometheus_targets
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, verify]

- name: Verify Loki is receiving logs
  command: >
    kubectl exec -n service deploy/loki --
    wget -qO- http://localhost:3100/ready
  register: loki_health
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, observability, verify]

- name: Observability deployment summary
  debug:
    msg:
      - "========================================="
      - "Observability Stack Deployment Complete"
      - "========================================="
      - "Monitoring:"
      - "  Prometheus: {{ prometheus_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Grafana: {{ grafana_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Alertmanager: {{ alertmanager_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "Logging:"
      - "  Loki: {{ loki_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "  Promtail: {{ promtail_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "========================================="
      - "Access URLs (via Traefik ingress):"
      - "  Grafana: https://grafana.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }}"
      - "  Prometheus: https://prometheus.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }}"
      - "========================================="
  tags: [infrastructure, observability]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/infrastructure/tasks/post_deploy.yml">
---
# Post-deployment tasks
# Extracts credentials from deployed services and generates deployment report

- name: Display post-deployment plan
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Running post-deployment tasks"
      - "1. Extract service credentials"
      - "2. Generate deployment report"
      - "3. Display access URLs"
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy]

# ============================================================
# CREDENTIAL EXTRACTION
# ============================================================

# Vault - root token is already saved during init (vault_unseal.yml)
- name: Check for existing Vault keys file
  find:
    paths: "{{ lookup('env', 'HOME') }}"
    patterns: ".vault-keys-*.txt"
  delegate_to: localhost
  register: vault_keys_files
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Read latest Vault keys file
  slurp:
    src: "{{ (vault_keys_files.files | sort(attribute='mtime') | last).path }}"
  delegate_to: localhost
  register: vault_keys_content
  when: vault_keys_files.files | length > 0
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Parse Vault root token
  set_fact:
    vault_root_token: "{{ (vault_keys_content.content | b64decode | regex_search('Root Token: (.+)', '\\1')) | first }}"
  when: vault_keys_content is defined and vault_keys_content.content is defined
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

# Authentik - extract bootstrap token from pod logs
- name: Get Authentik server pod name
  command: >
    kubectl get pods -n service -l app.kubernetes.io/name=authentik,app.kubernetes.io/component=server
    -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}'
  register: authentik_pod
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Extract Authentik bootstrap token from logs
  command: >
    kubectl logs -n service {{ authentik_pod.stdout }} --tail=1000
  register: authentik_logs
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  when: authentik_pod.stdout != ""
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Parse Authentik bootstrap password
  set_fact:
    authentik_bootstrap_password: "{{ authentik_logs.stdout | regex_search('Initial admin password: ([^\\s]+)', '\\1') | default(['not-found']) | first }}"
  when: authentik_logs is defined and authentik_logs.stdout is defined
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

# Alternative: Extract from environment or secret
- name: Get Authentik secret
  command: >
    kubectl get secret -n service authentik -o jsonpath='{.data.AUTHENTIK_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD}'
  register: authentik_secret
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Decode Authentik bootstrap password from secret
  set_fact:
    authentik_bootstrap_password: "{{ authentik_secret.stdout | b64decode }}"
  when:
    - authentik_secret is defined
    - authentik_secret.stdout is defined
    - authentik_secret.stdout != ""
    - authentik_bootstrap_password is not defined or authentik_bootstrap_password == "not-found"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

# Grafana - extract admin password from secret
- name: Get Grafana admin password
  command: >
    kubectl get secret -n service grafana -o jsonpath='{.data.admin-password}'
  register: grafana_secret
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Decode Grafana admin password
  set_fact:
    grafana_admin_password: "{{ grafana_secret.stdout | b64decode }}"
  when: grafana_secret is defined and grafana_secret.stdout is defined and grafana_secret.stdout != ""
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

# GitLab - extract root password (if deployed)
- name: Get GitLab root password
  command: >
    kubectl get secret -n code gitlab-gitlab-initial-root-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'
  register: gitlab_secret
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

- name: Decode GitLab root password
  set_fact:
    gitlab_root_password: "{{ gitlab_secret.stdout | b64decode }}"
  when: gitlab_secret is defined and gitlab_secret.stdout is defined and gitlab_secret.stdout != ""
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, credentials]

# ============================================================
# GATHER SERVICE URLS
# ============================================================

- name: Get all ingress resources
  command: kubectl get ingress -A -o json
  register: all_ingresses
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, urls]

- name: Parse ingress URLs
  set_fact:
    service_urls: "{{ (all_ingresses.stdout | from_json).items | map(attribute='spec.rules') | flatten | map(attribute='host') | list | unique }}"
  when: all_ingresses is defined and all_ingresses.stdout is defined
  failed_when: false
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, urls]

# ============================================================
# GENERATE DEPLOYMENT REPORT
# ============================================================

- name: Set deployment report path
  set_fact:
    deployment_report_path: "{{ lookup('env', 'HOME') }}/.selfhost-deployment-report.txt"
  tags: [infrastructure, post-deploy, report]

- name: Generate deployment report
  copy:
    content: |
      =========================================
      SELF-HOSTED DEPLOYMENT REPORT
      Generated: {{ ansible_date_time.iso8601 }}
      =========================================

      ## CREDENTIALS

      VAULT
        Status: Initialized and Unsealed
        Root Token: {{ vault_root_token | default('See ~/.vault-keys-*.txt') }}
        Unseal Keys: Stored in ~/.vault-keys-*.txt
        Note: Store these keys securely! You cannot recover them later.

      AUTHENTIK
        Admin User: akadmin
        Bootstrap Password: {{ authentik_bootstrap_password | default('Check pod logs or secret') }}
        Note: Change password on first login at https://authentik.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }}

      GRAFANA
        Admin User: admin
        Admin Password: {{ grafana_admin_password | default('Check grafana secret') }}

      {% if gitlab_root_password is defined and gitlab_root_password != '' %}
      GITLAB
        Root User: root
        Root Password: {{ gitlab_root_password }}
        Note: Change password on first login
      {% endif %}

      ## SERVICE URLs

      ### Local Access (*.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }})
      {% for url in service_urls | default([]) | select('search', domain.local | default('zeizel.local')) | list %}
        - https://{{ url }}
      {% endfor %}
      {% if service_urls | default([]) | select('search', domain.local | default('zeizel.local')) | list | length == 0 %}
        - https://vault.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }}
        - https://authentik.{{ domain.local | default('zeizel.local') }}

        - https://{{ url }}
      {% endfor %}

        - https://vault.{{ domain.public | default('zeizel.ru') }}
        - https://authentik.{{ domain.public | default('zeizel.ru') }}
      {% endif %}

      ## MANUAL SETUP REQUIRED

      1. VAULT
         - Status: Already unsealed, root token saved
         - Action: Configure authentication methods and secret engines

      2. AUTHENTIK
         - Status: Running with bootstrap token
         - Action: Login with akadmin and change password
         - Action: Configure OAuth providers for other services

      3. DNS
         - Action: Add A records for *.{{ domain.public | default('zeizel.ru') }} -> {{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_host'] | default('VPS_IP') }}

      4. LOCAL ACCESS
         - Run: selfhost local-access
         - Or manually add entries to /etc/hosts pointing to Traefik IP

      ## INFRASTRUCTURE STATUS

      Pangolin VPN: {{ pangolin_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Connected', 'Check manually') }}
      Storage (OpenEBS): {{ openebs_ready.rc | default(1) == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Check manually') }}

{% endif %}

<file path="docker/authn/docker-compose.yml">
services:
  postgresql:
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${PG_DB:-authentik}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${PG_PASS:?database password required}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${PG_USER:-authentik}
    healthcheck:
      interval: 30s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 20s
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - pg_isready -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER}
      timeout: 5s
    image: docker.io/library/postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - database:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  redis:
    command: --save 60 1 --loglevel warning
    healthcheck:
      interval: 30s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 20s
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - redis-cli ping | grep PONG
      timeout: 3s
    image: docker.io/library/redis:alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - redis:/data

  server:
    command: server
    depends_on:
      postgresql:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__HOST: postgresql
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__NAME: ${PG_DB:-authentik}
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__PASSWORD: ${PG_PASS}
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__USER: ${PG_USER:-authentik}
      AUTHENTIK_REDIS__HOST: redis
      AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY: ${AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY:?secret key required}
    image: ${AUTHENTIK_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/goauthentik/server}:${AUTHENTIK_TAG:-2025.8.4}
    ports:
      - ${COMPOSE_PORT_HTTP:-9000}:9000
      - ${COMPOSE_PORT_HTTPS:-9443}:9443
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./media:/media
      - ./custom-templates:/templates

  worker:
    command: worker
    depends_on:
      postgresql:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__HOST: postgresql
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__NAME: ${PG_DB:-authentik}
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__PASSWORD: ${PG_PASS}
      AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__USER: ${PG_USER:-authentik}
      AUTHENTIK_REDIS__HOST: redis
      AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY: ${AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY:?secret key required}
    image: ${AUTHENTIK_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/goauthentik/server}:${AUTHENTIK_TAG:-2025.8.4}
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: root
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - ./media:/media
      - ./certs:/certs
      - ./custom-templates:/templates

volumes:
  database:
    driver: local
  redis:
    driver: local
</file>

      - broker-net

      interval: 10s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s

  data:

<file path="docs/src/components/HomepageFeatures/index.tsx">
import type {ReactNode} from 'react';
import clsx from 'clsx';
import Heading from '@theme/Heading';
import styles from './styles.module.css';

type FeatureItem = {
  title: string;
  Svg: React.ComponentType<React.ComponentProps<'svg'>>;
  description: ReactNode;
};

const FeatureList: FeatureItem[] = [
  {
    title: 'Easy to Use',
    Svg: require('@site/static/img/undraw_docusaurus_mountain.svg').default,
    description: (
      <>
        Docusaurus was designed from the ground up to be easily installed and
        used to get your website up and running quickly.
      </>
    ),
  },
  {
    title: 'Focus on What Matters',
    Svg: require('@site/static/img/undraw_docusaurus_tree.svg').default,
    description: (
      <>
        Docusaurus lets you focus on your docs, and we&apos;ll do the chores. Go
        ahead and move your docs into the <code>docs</code> directory.
      </>
    ),
  },
  {
    title: 'Powered by React',
    Svg: require('@site/static/img/undraw_docusaurus_react.svg').default,
    description: (
      <>
        Extend or customize your website layout by reusing React. Docusaurus can
        be extended while reusing the same header and footer.
      </>
    ),
  },
];

function Feature({title, Svg, description}: FeatureItem) {
  return (
    <div className={clsx('col col--4')}>
      <div className="text--center">
        <Svg className={styles.featureSvg} role="img" />
      </div>
      <div className="text--center padding-horiz--md">
        <Heading as="h3">{title}</Heading>
        <p>{description}</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

export default function HomepageFeatures(): ReactNode {
  return (
    <section className={styles.features}>
      <div className="container">
        <div className="row">
          {FeatureList.map((props, idx) => (
            <Feature key={idx} {...props} />
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>
  );
}
</file>

<file path="docs/src/components/HomepageFeatures/styles.module.css">

  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 2rem 0;
  width: 100%;
}

.featureSvg {
  height: 200px;
  width: 200px;
}
</file>
