<file path="ansible/roles/kubespray/tasks/join-gateway.yml">
---
# =============================================================================
# KUBESPRAY JOIN GATEWAY TASKS
# =============================================================================
# Add VPS gateway node to existing Kubernetes cluster and label it

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-Join Checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Verify kubespray scale.yml exists
  stat:
    path: "{{ kubespray_dir }}/scale.yml"
  register: scale_yml_stat
  failed_when: not scale_yml_stat.stat.exists
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

- name: Verify inventory includes gateway node
  assert:
    that:
      - groups['gateway'] is defined
      - groups['gateway'] | length > 0
    fail_msg: "Gateway group must be defined in inventory with at least one host"
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

- name: Verify VPS is reachable
  wait_for:
    host: "{{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_host'] }}"
    port: "{{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_port'] | default(22) }}"
    timeout: 30
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Regenerate Inventory with Gateway
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Regenerate kubespray inventory to include gateway
  template:
    src: inventory.yaml.j2
    dest: "{{ kubespray_inventory_dir }}/hosts.yaml"
    mode: '0644'
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, inventory]

- name: Display gateway join message
  debug:
    msg: |
      Joining VPS gateway node to Kubernetes cluster...

      Gateway node: {{ groups['gateway'][0] }}
      Gateway IP: {{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_host'] }}

      This will use kubespray scale.yml to add the node.
      The node will be labeled as a gateway node.
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Join Gateway Node
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Run kubespray scale.yml to join gateway node
  shell: |
    source {{ kubespray_venv_dir }}/bin/activate
    cd {{ kubespray_dir }}
    ansible-playbook -i {{ kubespray_inventory_dir }}/hosts.yaml \
      scale.yml \
      --become \
      --become-user={{ kubespray_become_user }} \
      -v
  args:
    executable: /bin/bash
  environment:
    ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING: "False"
    ANSIBLE_DEPRECATION_WARNINGS: "False"
    ANSIBLE_CALLBACKS_ENABLED: ""
  register: kubespray_scale_gateway
  async: "{{ kubespray_deploy_timeout }}"
  poll: "{{ kubespray_poll_interval }}"
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

- name: Display scale output
  debug:
    msg: "{{ kubespray_scale_gateway.stdout_lines[-50:] }}"
  when: kubespray_scale_gateway.stdout_lines is defined
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

- name: Check scale result
  fail:
    msg: |
      Failed to join gateway node to cluster!

      Error: {{ kubespray_scale_gateway.stderr | default('Unknown error') }}

      Check the full output above for details.
  when: kubespray_scale_gateway.rc != 0
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, join]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wait for Node to be Ready
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Wait for gateway node to appear in cluster
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    get node {{ groups['gateway'][0] }} -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'
  register: gateway_node_status
  changed_when: false
  retries: 60
  delay: 10
  until: gateway_node_status.stdout == "True"
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, verify]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Label Gateway Node
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Label gateway node with gateway role
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    label node {{ groups['gateway'][0] }}
    node-role.kubernetes.io/gateway=true
    --overwrite
  register: label_gateway
  changed_when: "'labeled' in label_gateway.stdout or 'not labeled' not in label_gateway.stdout"
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, label]

- name: Taint gateway node to prevent regular workloads
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    taint node {{ groups['gateway'][0] }}
    node-role.kubernetes.io/gateway=true:NoSchedule
    --overwrite
  register: taint_gateway
  changed_when: "'tainted' in taint_gateway.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, taint]
  when: kubespray_gateway_taint | default(true)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify Gateway Node
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Get gateway node info
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    get node {{ groups['gateway'][0] }} -o wide
  register: gateway_node_info
  changed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, verify]

- name: Display gateway node status
  debug:
    msg: |
      =====================================================
      Gateway Node Joined Successfully!
      =====================================================

      Node: {{ groups['gateway'][0] }}
      {{ gateway_node_info.stdout }}

      Labels applied:
        - node-role.kubernetes.io/gateway=true

      {% if kubespray_gateway_taint | default(true) %}
      Taints applied:
        - node-role.kubernetes.io/gateway=true:NoSchedule
      {% endif %}

      Next steps:
        1. Deploy pangolin-server chart to this node
        2. Configure VPN tunnel

      Command to verify:
        kubectl get nodes -l node-role.kubernetes.io/gateway=true
      =====================================================
  tags: [kubespray, gateway, summary]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/kubespray/tasks/post-install.yml">
---
# =============================================================================
# KUBESPRAY POST-INSTALL TASKS
# =============================================================================
# Install essential cluster components after Kubernetes deployment
# - local-path-provisioner (storage)
# - Traefik (ingress controller)
# - Required namespaces

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Namespaces
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Create required namespaces
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    create namespace {{ item }} --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
  loop:
    - ingress
    - service
    - db
    - infrastructure
    - code
    - productivity
    - social
    - data
    - automation
    - content
    - utilities
  changed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, namespaces]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Storage: local-path-provisioner
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy local-path-provisioner
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/local-path-provisioner/v{{ local_path_provisioner_version }}/deploy/local-path-storage.yaml
  register: storage_deploy
  changed_when: "'created' in storage_deploy.stdout or 'configured' in storage_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, storage]

- name: Wait for local-path-provisioner to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    get pods -n local-path-storage -l app=local-path-provisioner
    -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.phase}'
  register: storage_status
  changed_when: false
  retries: 30
  delay: 5
  until: "'Running' in storage_status.stdout"
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, storage]

- name: Set local-path as default StorageClass
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} patch storageclass local-path \
      -p '{"metadata": {"annotations":{"storageclass.kubernetes.io/is-default-class":"true"}}}'
  changed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, storage]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ingress: Traefik
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Add Traefik Helm repository
  command: >
    helm repo add traefik https://traefik.github.io/charts
  register: helm_repo_add
  changed_when: "'has been added' in helm_repo_add.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, traefik]

- name: Update Helm repositories
  command: helm repo update
  changed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, traefik]

- name: Deploy Traefik with NodePort
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install traefik traefik/traefik
    --namespace ingress
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set service.type=NodePort
    --set ports.web.nodePort={{ traefik_nodeport_http }}
    --set ports.websecure.nodePort={{ traefik_nodeport_https }}
    --set ports.web.exposedPort=80
    --set ports.websecure.exposedPort=443
    --set ingressRoute.dashboard.enabled=false
    --set providers.kubernetesIngress.enabled=true
    --set providers.kubernetesCRD.enabled=true
    --set logs.general.level=INFO
    --set ping.entryPoint=web
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: traefik_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in traefik_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in traefik_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, traefik]

- name: Wait for Traefik to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    get pods -n ingress -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
    -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.phase}'
  register: traefik_status
  changed_when: false
  retries: 30
  delay: 5
  until: "'Running' in traefik_status.stdout"
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, traefik]

- name: Get Traefik NodePort info
  command: >
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    get svc traefik -n ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[*].nodePort}'
  register: traefik_ports
  changed_when: false
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, traefik]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Post-install Summary
  debug:
    msg: |
      =====================================================
      Kubernetes Post-Install Complete!
      =====================================================

      Storage:
        - local-path-provisioner: deployed (default StorageClass)

      Ingress:
        - Traefik: deployed in ingress namespace
        - NodePort HTTP: {{ traefik_nodeport_http }}
        - NodePort HTTPS: {{ traefik_nodeport_https }}

      Namespaces created:
        - ingress, service, db, infrastructure
        - code, productivity, social, data
        - automation, content, utilities

      Next steps:
        1. Configure VPN (pangolin role)
        2. Deploy applications via Helmfile
      =====================================================
  tags: [kubespray, post-install, summary]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/kubespray/tasks/scale.yml">
---
# =============================================================================
# KUBESPRAY SCALE TASKS
# =============================================================================
# Add new nodes to existing Kubernetes cluster

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-scale Checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Verify kubespray directory exists
  stat:
    path: "{{ kubespray_dir }}/scale.yml"
  register: scale_yml_stat
  failed_when: not scale_yml_stat.stat.exists

- name: Verify inventory exists
  stat:
    path: "{{ kubespray_inventory_dir }}/hosts.yaml"
  register: inventory_stat
  failed_when: not inventory_stat.stat.exists

- name: Verify virtualenv exists
  stat:
    path: "{{ kubespray_venv_dir }}/bin/ansible-playbook"
  register: ansible_playbook_stat
  failed_when: not ansible_playbook_stat.stat.exists

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Scale Cluster (Add New Nodes)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Display scale start message
  debug:
    msg: |
      Starting Kubernetes cluster scale operation...

      This will add new nodes to the cluster.

      Playbook: {{ kubespray_dir }}/scale.yml
      Inventory: {{ kubespray_inventory_dir }}/hosts.yaml

      Current nodes in inventory:
        Masters: {{ groups['masters'] | default([]) | join(', ') }}
        Workers: {{ groups['workers'] | default([]) | join(', ') }}
        Storages: {{ groups.get('storages', []) | join(', ') }}

# Note: scale.yml must run WITHOUT --limit because it needs facts from all nodes
# Kubespray will detect which nodes are already in the cluster and skip them
- name: Run kubespray scale.yml playbook
  shell: |
    source {{ kubespray_venv_dir }}/bin/activate
    cd {{ kubespray_dir }}
    ansible-playbook -i {{ kubespray_inventory_dir }}/hosts.yaml \
      scale.yml \
      --become \
      --become-user={{ kubespray_become_user }} \
      -v
  args:
    executable: /bin/bash
  environment:
    ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING: "False"
    ANSIBLE_DEPRECATION_WARNINGS: "False"
    ANSIBLE_CALLBACKS_ENABLED: ""
  register: kubespray_scale
  async: "{{ kubespray_deploy_timeout }}"
  poll: "{{ kubespray_poll_interval }}"

- name: Display scale output
  debug:
    msg: "{{ kubespray_scale.stdout_lines[-50:] }}"
  when: kubespray_scale.stdout_lines is defined

- name: Check scale result
  fail:
    msg: |
      Kubespray scale operation failed!

      Error: {{ kubespray_scale.stderr | default('Unknown error') }}

      Check the full output above for details.
  when: kubespray_scale.rc != 0

- name: Verify new nodes joined cluster
  shell: |
    kubectl get nodes -o wide
  register: nodes_after_scale
  changed_when: false

- name: Display cluster nodes after scale
  debug:
    msg: "{{ nodes_after_scale.stdout_lines }}"

- name: Scale complete
  debug:
    msg: |
      Kubernetes cluster scale completed!

      New nodes have been added to the cluster.

      Verify with: kubectl get nodes
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/kubespray/tasks/services.yml">
---
# =============================================================================
# KUBERNETES SERVICES DEPLOYMENT
# =============================================================================
# Deploy all applications to Kubernetes cluster via Helm
#
# Order:
#   1. Core infrastructure (cert-manager, consul, vault)
#   2. Databases (PostgreSQL, Valkey, MongoDB, MinIO)
#   3. Authentication (Authentik)
#   4. Applications (all other services)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prerequisites
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Ensure Helm repositories are configured
  command: helm repo add {{ item.name }} {{ item.url }}
  loop:
    - { name: "jetstack", url: "https://charts.jetstack.io" }
    - { name: "bitnami", url: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami" }
    - { name: "hashicorp", url: "https://helm.releases.hashicorp.com" }
    - { name: "authentik", url: "https://charts.goauthentik.io" }
    - { name: "traefik", url: "https://traefik.github.io/charts" }
    - { name: "gitlab", url: "https://charts.gitlab.io/" }
  register: helm_repos
  changed_when: "'has been added' in helm_repos.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, repos]

- name: Update Helm repositories
  command: helm repo update
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, repos]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cert-manager
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy cert-manager
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager
    --namespace cert-manager
    --create-namespace
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --version v1.17.1
    --set crds.enabled=true
    --set crds.keep=true
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: certmanager_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in certmanager_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in certmanager_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, cert-manager]

- name: Create ClusterIssuers
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} apply -f - <<EOF
    apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
    kind: ClusterIssuer
    metadata:
      name: letsencrypt-prod
    spec:
      acme:
        server: https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
        email: admin@{{ domain.public }}
        privateKeySecretRef:
          name: letsencrypt-prod
        solvers:
        - http01:
            ingress:
              class: traefik
    ---
    apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
    kind: ClusterIssuer
    metadata:
      name: selfsigned-issuer
    spec:
      selfSigned: {}
    EOF
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, cert-manager]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Databases
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Create PostgreSQL init database ConfigMap
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} apply -f - <<EOF
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: ConfigMap
    metadata:
      name: scripts-initdb
      namespace: db
    data:
      init.sql: |
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS authentik;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS vaultwarden;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS gitlab;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS harbor;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS teamcity;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS youtrack;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS hub;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS coder;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS affine;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS nextcloud;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS kestra;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS n8n;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS ghost;
        CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS bytebase;
    EOF
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, databases, postgres]

- name: Deploy PostgreSQL
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install postgresql bitnami/postgresql
    --namespace db
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set global.storageClass=local-path
    --set auth.enablePostgresUser=true
    --set auth.postgresPassword={{ database_password }}
    --set architecture=standalone
    --set primary.persistence.enabled=true
    --set primary.persistence.size=8Gi
    --set primary.initdb.scriptsConfigMap=scripts-initdb
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: postgres_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in postgres_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in postgres_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, databases, postgres]

- name: Deploy Valkey (Redis)
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install valkey bitnami/valkey
    --namespace db
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set global.storageClass=local-path
    --set auth.password={{ valkey_password }}
    --set architecture=standalone
    --set primary.persistence.enabled=true
    --set primary.persistence.size=2Gi
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: valkey_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in valkey_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in valkey_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, databases, valkey]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vault
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy HashiCorp Vault
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install vault hashicorp/vault
    --namespace service
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set server.dev.enabled=true
    --set server.dataStorage.enabled=false
    --set injector.enabled=true
    --set ui.enabled=true
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: vault_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in vault_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in vault_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, vault]

- name: Create Vault Ingress
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} apply -f - <<EOF
    apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
    kind: Ingress
    metadata:
      name: vault
      namespace: service
    spec:
      ingressClassName: traefik
      rules:
      - host: vault.{{ domain.local }}
        http:
          paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: vault
                port:
                  number: 8200
    EOF
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, vault]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authentik (SSO)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy Authentik
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install authentik authentik/authentik
    --namespace service
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set authentik.secret_key={{ authentik_secret_key }}
    --set authentik.postgresql.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set authentik.postgresql.name=authentik
    --set authentik.postgresql.user=postgres
    --set authentik.postgresql.password={{ database_password }}
    --set authentik.redis.host=valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set authentik.redis.password={{ valkey_password }}
    --set authentik.bootstrapPassword={{ authentik_admin_password }}
    --set server.replicas=1
    --set worker.replicas=1
    --set postgresql.enabled=false
    --set redis.enabled=false
    --wait
    --timeout=10m
  register: authentik_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in authentik_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in authentik_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, authentik]

- name: Create Authentik Ingress
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} apply -f - <<EOF
    apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
    kind: Ingress
    metadata:
      name: authentik
      namespace: service
    spec:
      ingressClassName: traefik
      rules:
      - host: auth.{{ domain.local }}
        http:
          paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: authentik-server
                port:
                  number: 80
    EOF
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, authentik]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom Charts (from kubernetes/charts/)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy Glance Dashboard
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install glance {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/glance
    --namespace infrastructure
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=dashboard.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.config.storageClass=local-path
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: glance_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in glance_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in glance_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, glance]

- name: Deploy Vaultwarden
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install vaultwarden {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/vaultwarden
    --namespace data
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=vw.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --set postgresql.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set postgresql.password={{ database_password }}
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: vaultwarden_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in vaultwarden_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in vaultwarden_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, vaultwarden]

- name: Deploy Excalidraw
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install excalidraw {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/excalidraw
    --namespace productivity
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.frontend.host=excalidraw.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.data.storageClass=local-path
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: excalidraw_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in excalidraw_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in excalidraw_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, excalidraw]

- name: Deploy MeTube
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install metube {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/metube
    --namespace utilities
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=metube.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: metube_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in metube_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in metube_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, metube]

- name: Deploy Bytebase
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install bytebase {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/bytebase
    --namespace infrastructure
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=bytebase.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --set bytebase.postgres.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set bytebase.postgres.password={{ database_password }}
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: bytebase_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in bytebase_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in bytebase_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, bytebase]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# JetBrains Stack
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy Hub
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install hub {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/hub
    --namespace code
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=hub.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: hub_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in hub_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in hub_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, hub, jetbrains]

- name: Deploy YouTrack
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install youtrack {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/youtrack
    --namespace code
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=youtrack.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: youtrack_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in youtrack_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in youtrack_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, youtrack, jetbrains]

- name: Deploy TeamCity
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install teamcity {{ kubernetes_charts_dir }}/teamcity
    --namespace code
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set ingress.host=teamcity.{{ domain.local }}
    --set ingress.tls.enabled=false
    --set consul.enabled=false
    --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
    --set serviceMonitor.enabled=false
    --set persistence.storageClass=local-path
    --set postgresql.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set postgresql.password={{ database_password }}
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: teamcity_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in teamcity_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in teamcity_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, teamcity, jetbrains]

    --set ingress.hosts[0].paths[0].pathType=Prefix

- name: Deploy Penpot

    helm repo add penpot https://helm.penpot.app;
    helm upgrade --install penpot penpot/penpot
    --namespace productivity
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set config.publicUri=https://penpot.{{ domain.public }}
    --set config.flags="enable-registration enable-login-with-password disable-email-verification"
    --set ingress.enabled=true
    --set ingress.className=traefik
    --set ingress.hosts[0]=penpot.{{ domain.local }}
    --set global.postgresqlEnabled=false
    --set global.valkeyEnabled=false
    --set config.postgresql.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set config.postgresql.password={{ database_password }}
    --set config.valkey.host=valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: penpot_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in penpot_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in penpot_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, penpot]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Infrastructure (Harbor)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy Harbor
  shell: >
    helm repo add harbor https://helm.goharbor.io;
    helm upgrade --install harbor harbor/harbor
    --namespace infrastructure
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set expose.type=ingress
    --set expose.ingress.className=traefik
    --set expose.ingress.hosts.core=harbor.{{ domain.local }}
    --set externalURL=https://harbor.{{ domain.public }}
    --set persistence.enabled=true
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.registry.storageClass=local-path
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.registry.size=50Gi
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.jobservice.storageClass=local-path
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.database.storageClass=local-path
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.redis.storageClass=local-path
    --set persistence.persistentVolumeClaim.trivy.storageClass=local-path
    --set harborAdminPassword=Harbor12345
    --set database.type=external
    --set database.external.host=postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
    --set database.external.username=harbor
    --set database.external.password={{ database_password }}
    --set redis.type=external
    --set redis.external.addr=valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local:6379
    --wait
    --timeout=10m
  register: harbor_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in harbor_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in harbor_deploy.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [services, harbor]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monitoring
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Deploy Grafana
  command: >
    helm upgrade --install grafana bitnami/grafana
    --namespace service
    --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }}
    --set global.storageClass=local-path
    --set admin.password={{ grafana_password }}
    --set persistence.enabled=true
    --set persistence.size=5Gi
    --wait
    --timeout=5m
  register: grafana_deploy
  changed_when: "'deployed' in grafana_deploy.stdout or 'upgraded' in grafana_deploy.stdout"
  tags: [services, monitoring, grafana]

- name: Create Grafana Ingress
  shell: |
    kubectl --kubeconfig={{ kubespray_local_kubeconfig }} apply -f - <<EOF
    apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
    kind: Ingress
    metadata:
      name: grafana
      namespace: service
    spec:
      ingressClassName: traefik
      rules:
      - host: monitoring.{{ domain.local }}
        http:
          paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: grafana
                port:
                  number: 3000
    EOF
  changed_when: false
  tags: [services, monitoring, grafana]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Services Deployment Summary
  debug:
    msg: |
      =====================================================
      Services Deployment Complete!
      =====================================================

      Core Services:
        - cert-manager: deployed
        - Vault: vault.{{ domain.local }}
        - Authentik: auth.{{ domain.local }}

      Databases:
        - PostgreSQL: postgresql.db.svc.cluster.local
        - Valkey: valkey-master.db.svc.cluster.local

      Applications:
        - Glance: dashboard.{{ domain.local }}

  memory.available: "100Mi"

<file path="ansible/roles/kubespray/README.md">
# Kubespray Role

Deploys Kubernetes cluster using Kubespray.

## Overview

This role:
- Clones Kubespray repository
- Configures cluster inventory
- Deploys single-node or multi-node Kubernetes cluster
- Configures kubectl on master node

## Requirements

- Ubuntu 20.04+ on target nodes
- SSH access with sudo privileges
- Python 3 installed on targets

## Role Variables

See `defaults/main.yml` for key variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `kubespray_version` | Kubespray git tag/branch |
| `kubespray_dir` | Local directory for Kubespray |
| `kube_version` | Kubernetes version to deploy |
| `kube_network_plugin` | CNI plugin (calico, flannel, etc.) |
| `cluster_name` | Kubernetes cluster name |

## Cluster Configuration

Default configuration:
- Kubernetes v1.28.5
- Calico CNI with IPIP encapsulation
- etcd cluster (co-located with control plane)
- containerd runtime

## Dependencies

- Docker role (containerd runtime)
- Network connectivity between nodes

## Example Playbook

```yaml
- hosts: k8s_masters:k8s_workers
  roles:
    - kubespray
  tags: [kubespray]
```

## Tags

- `kubespray` - Run all Kubespray tasks
- `kubespray, prepare` - Prepare nodes for deployment
- `kubespray, deploy` - Run cluster deployment

## Post-Deployment

After deployment:
1. Copy kubeconfig to local machine
2. Verify cluster status with `kubectl get nodes`
3. Configure CNI (if separate role)
4. Deploy storage provisioner

## Scaling

To add worker nodes:
1. Add to `[k8s_workers]` in inventory
2. Re-run playbook with `--tags kubespray`
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/local-access/defaults/main.yml">
---
# Local access role defaults
# Configures /etc/hosts on the local machine for cluster service access

# Enable local access configuration
local_access_enabled: true

# Local domain suffix for cluster services
local_domain: "zeizel.local"

# Traefik node IP (where ingress controller runs)
traefik_node_ip: "192.168.100.100"

# Traefik NodePort for HTTP traffic
traefik_nodeport_http: 30080
traefik_nodeport_https: 30443

# Marker for hosts file entries (for idempotent updates)
hosts_marker: "# Kubernetes cluster services ({{ local_domain }})"

# Services to configure for local access
# These will be fetched from cluster ingresses if auto_discover is true
local_access_services: []

# Auto-discover services from cluster ingresses
auto_discover_services: true

# Additional custom services (always added)
custom_local_services:
  - "dashboard"
  - "glance"
  - "monitoring"
  - "vault"
  - "auth"
  - "zerobyte"
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/local-access/tasks/main.yml">
---
# =============================================================================
# LOCAL ACCESS CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================
# Configures /etc/hosts on local machine for cluster service access
# This allows accessing services via <service>.zeizel.local

- name: Display local access configuration
  debug:
    msg:
      - "========================================="
      - "Configuring Local Access"
      - "========================================="
      - "Local domain: {{ local_domain }}"
      - "Traefik IP: {{ traefik_node_ip }}"
      - "Auto-discover: {{ auto_discover_services }}"
      - "========================================="
  tags: [local-access, info]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Auto-discover services from cluster ingresses
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Get ingresses with local domain
  shell: |
    kubectl get ingress -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .spec.rules[*]}{.host}{"\n"}{end}{end}' | \
    grep "{{ local_domain }}" | \
    sed 's/\.{{ local_domain | regex_escape }}$//' | \
    sort -u
  register: discovered_services
  changed_when: false
  when: auto_discover_services
  tags: [local-access, discover]

- name: Set discovered services list
  set_fact:
    all_local_services: "{{ (discovered_services.stdout_lines | default([])) + custom_local_services | unique }}"
  tags: [local-access, discover]

- name: Display services to configure
  debug:
    msg: "Services for local access: {{ all_local_services | join(', ') }}"
  tags: [local-access, info]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build hosts entries
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Build hosts file entries
  set_fact:
    hosts_entries: "{{ traefik_node_ip }} {{ all_local_services | map('regex_replace', '$', '.' + local_domain) | join(' ') }}"
  tags: [local-access, hosts]

- name: Display hosts entries
  debug:
    msg: "Hosts entry: {{ hosts_entries }}"
  tags: [local-access, info]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Update /etc/hosts (with fallback for passwordless sudo)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Check if marker exists in /etc/hosts
  shell: grep -q "{{ hosts_marker }}" /etc/hosts
  register: marker_check
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [local-access, hosts]

- name: Generate hosts update script
  copy:
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      # Auto-generated script to update /etc/hosts for local cluster access
      # Run with: sudo ./update-hosts.sh

      MARKER="{{ hosts_marker }}"
      ENTRIES="{{ hosts_entries }}"

      # Remove old entries if marker exists
      if grep -q "$MARKER" /etc/hosts; then
        echo "Removing old entries..."
        sed -i.bak "/$MARKER/d" /etc/hosts
        sed -i.bak "/{{ traefik_node_ip }}.*{{ local_domain }}/d" /etc/hosts
      fi

      # Add new entries
      echo "Adding new entries..."
      echo "" >> /etc/hosts
      echo "$MARKER" >> /etc/hosts
      echo "$ENTRIES" >> /etc/hosts

      echo "Done! Hosts file updated."
      grep "{{ local_domain }}" /etc/hosts
    dest: "/tmp/update-hosts.sh"
    mode: '0755'
  tags: [local-access, hosts]

- name: Try to update /etc/hosts directly
  shell: |
    # Check if we can write to /etc/hosts
    if sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then
      # Remove old entries
      sudo sed -i.bak '/{{ hosts_marker | regex_escape }}/d' /etc/hosts 2>/dev/null || true
      sudo sed -i.bak '/{{ traefik_node_ip }}.*{{ local_domain }}/d' /etc/hosts 2>/dev/null || true
      # Add new entries
      echo "" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts > /dev/null
      echo "{{ hosts_marker }}" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts > /dev/null
      echo "{{ hosts_entries }}" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts > /dev/null
      echo "SUCCESS"
    else
      echo "NEEDS_SUDO"
    fi
  register: hosts_update
  changed_when: "'SUCCESS' in hosts_update.stdout"
  failed_when: false
  tags: [local-access, hosts]

- name: Display manual instruction if sudo required
  debug:
    msg:
      - "========================================="
      - "MANUAL ACTION REQUIRED"
      - "========================================="
      - "Run the following command to update /etc/hosts:"
      - ""
      - "  sudo /tmp/update-hosts.sh"
      - ""
      - "Or manually add these lines to /etc/hosts:"
      - "  {{ hosts_marker }}"
      - "  {{ hosts_entries }}"
      - "========================================="
  when: "'NEEDS_SUDO' in hosts_update.stdout"
  tags: [local-access, hosts]

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
- name: Verify hosts file entries
  shell: grep "{{ local_domain }}" /etc/hosts
  register: hosts_verify
  changed_when: false
  tags: [local-access, verify]

- name: Display hosts file entries
  debug:
    msg: "{{ hosts_verify.stdout_lines }}"
  tags: [local-access, verify]

- name: Local access configuration complete
  debug:
    msg:
      - "========================================="
      - "Local Access Configured!"
      - "========================================="
      - ""
      - "IMPORTANT: Use port {{ traefik_nodeport_http }} for HTTP access"
      - ""
      - "Access services via curl with Host header:"
      - "  curl -H 'Host: <app>.{{ local_domain }}' http://{{ traefik_node_ip }}:{{ traefik_nodeport_http }}/"
      - ""
      - "Or in browser (set up proxy to port {{ traefik_nodeport_http }}):"
      - "{% for svc in all_local_services %}  - http://{{ svc }}.{{ local_domain }}:{{ traefik_nodeport_http }}"
      - "{% endfor %}"
      - ""
      - "For standard port 80 access, set up local SSH tunnel:"
      - "  ssh -fNL 80:{{ traefik_node_ip }}:{{ traefik_nodeport_http }} proxmox"
      - "========================================="
  tags: [local-access]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/monitoring/defaults/main.yml">
---
# Monitoring role defaults
# Prometheus/Grafana stack verification and configuration

# Monitoring namespace
monitoring_namespace: "service"

# Prometheus configuration
prometheus_service_name: "prometheus-kube-prometheus-prometheus"
prometheus_port: 9090
prometheus_retention: "15d"
prometheus_storage_size: "50Gi"

# Grafana configuration
grafana_service_name: "prometheus-grafana"
grafana_port: 80
grafana_admin_user: "admin"

# Alertmanager configuration
alertmanager_service_name: "prometheus-kube-prometheus-alertmanager"
alertmanager_port: 9093

# ServiceMonitor label
servicemonitor_label: "prometheus: kube-prometheus"

# Dashboards to import
grafana_dashboards:
  - name: "Kubernetes Cluster"
    id: 7249
  - name: "Node Exporter Full"
    id: 1860
  - name: "Traefik"
    id: 11462
  - name: "PostgreSQL"
    id: 9628

# Alerting rules
alerting_rules_enabled: true
alerting_rules_namespace: "{{ monitoring_namespace }}"

# Alert routes
alert_routes:
  - name: "critical"
    matchers:
      - "severity = critical"
    receiver: "slack"
  - name: "warning"
    matchers:
      - "severity = warning"
    receiver: "slack"

# Scrape configuration
scrape_interval: "30s"
scrape_timeout: "10s"

# Verification timeout
verify_timeout: 300
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/monitoring/meta/main.yml">
galaxy_info:
  role_name: monitoring
  description: Prometheus/Grafana monitoring stack verification and configuration
  author: Lvov Valery
  license: MIT
  min_ansible_version: 2.20.1

  platforms:
    - name: Ubuntu
      versions:
        - jammy
        - focal

  galaxy_tags:
    - kubernetes
    - monitoring
    - prometheus
    - grafana
    - alerting
    - observability

dependencies: []
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/monitoring/tasks/main.yml">
---
# Monitoring role main tasks
# Verifies Prometheus/Grafana stack and imports dashboards

- name: Verify monitoring namespace exists
  command: kubectl get namespace {{ monitoring_namespace }}
  register: ns_check
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: ns_check.rc != 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify]

- name: Get Prometheus pods
  command: kubectl get pods -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
  register: prometheus_pods
  changed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, prometheus]

- name: Display Prometheus pods status
  debug:
    msg: "{{ prometheus_pods.stdout_lines }}"
  tags: [monitoring, verify, prometheus]

- name: Wait for Prometheus pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
    --timeout={{ verify_timeout }}s
  register: prometheus_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, prometheus]

- name: Check Prometheus service
  command: kubectl get svc -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} {{ prometheus_service_name }}
  register: prometheus_svc
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, prometheus]

- name: Display Prometheus service
  debug:
    msg: "{{ prometheus_svc.stdout_lines }}"
  when: prometheus_svc.rc == 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify, prometheus]

- name: Get Grafana pods
  command: kubectl get pods -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana
  register: grafana_pods
  changed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, grafana]

- name: Display Grafana pods status
  debug:
    msg: "{{ grafana_pods.stdout_lines }}"
  tags: [monitoring, verify, grafana]

- name: Wait for Grafana pods to be ready
  command: >
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready
    pods -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana
    --timeout={{ verify_timeout }}s
  register: grafana_ready
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, grafana]

- name: Check Grafana service
  command: kubectl get svc -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} {{ grafana_service_name }}
  register: grafana_svc
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, grafana]

- name: Display Grafana service
  debug:
    msg: "{{ grafana_svc.stdout_lines }}"
  when: grafana_svc.rc == 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify, grafana]

- name: Get Alertmanager pods
  command: kubectl get pods -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
  register: alertmanager_pods
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, alertmanager]

- name: Display Alertmanager pods status
  debug:
    msg: "{{ alertmanager_pods.stdout_lines }}"
  when: alertmanager_pods.rc == 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify, alertmanager]

- name: Get all ServiceMonitors
  command: kubectl get servicemonitor -A
  register: servicemonitors
  changed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, servicemonitor]

- name: Display ServiceMonitors
  debug:
    msg: "{{ servicemonitors.stdout_lines }}"
  tags: [monitoring, verify, servicemonitor]

- name: Count ServiceMonitors
  set_fact:
    servicemonitor_count: "{{ servicemonitors.stdout_lines | length - 1 }}"
  tags: [monitoring, verify, servicemonitor]

- name: Get PrometheusRules
  command: kubectl get prometheusrule -A
  register: prometheus_rules
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, rules]

- name: Display PrometheusRules
  debug:
    msg: "{{ prometheus_rules.stdout_lines }}"
  when: prometheus_rules.rc == 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify, rules]

- name: Check Prometheus targets
  command: >
    kubectl exec -n {{ monitoring_namespace }}
    prometheus-prometheus-kube-prometheus-prometheus-0
    -- wget -qO- http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets
  register: prometheus_targets
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  tags: [monitoring, verify, targets]

- name: Parse Prometheus targets
  set_fact:
    targets_data: "{{ prometheus_targets.stdout | from_json }}"
  when: prometheus_targets.rc == 0
  tags: [monitoring, verify, targets]

- name: Count active targets
  set_fact:
    active_targets: "{{ targets_data.data.activeTargets | length }}"
  when: targets_data is defined
  tags: [monitoring, verify, targets]

- name: Display active targets count
  debug:
    msg: "Prometheus active targets: {{ active_targets | default('N/A') }}"
  tags: [monitoring, verify, targets]

- name: Import Grafana dashboards
  block:
    - name: Get Grafana admin password
      command: >
        kubectl get secret -n {{ monitoring_namespace }}
        {{ grafana_service_name }}
        -o jsonpath='{.data.admin-password}'
      register: grafana_password_b64
      changed_when: false
      no_log: true

    - name: Decode Grafana password
      set_fact:
        grafana_password: "{{ grafana_password_b64.stdout | b64decode }}"
      no_log: true

    - name: Create Grafana dashboard import job
      template:
        src: dashboard-import-job.yaml.j2
        dest: /tmp/dashboard-import-job.yaml
        mode: '0644'

    - name: Apply dashboard import job
      command: kubectl apply -f /tmp/dashboard-import-job.yaml
      register: job_apply
      changed_when: "'created' in job_apply.stdout"

    - name: Wait for dashboard import job to complete
      command: >
        kubectl wait --for=condition=complete
        job/grafana-dashboard-import
        -n {{ monitoring_namespace }}
        --timeout=180s
      register: job_complete
      changed_when: false
      failed_when: false

    - name: Remove dashboard import job
      command: kubectl delete job grafana-dashboard-import -n {{ monitoring_namespace }} --ignore-not-found
      changed_when: false

    - name: Remove temporary job file
      file:
        path: /tmp/dashboard-import-job.yaml
        state: absent
  tags: [monitoring, dashboards]

- name: Create custom alerting rules
  block:
    - name: Create PrometheusRule for infrastructure alerts
      copy:
        content: |
          apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
          kind: PrometheusRule
          metadata:
            name: infrastructure-alerts
            namespace: {{ monitoring_namespace }}
            labels:
              prometheus: kube-prometheus
          spec:
            groups:
              - name: infrastructure
                interval: 30s
                rules:
                  - alert: NodeDown
                    expr: up{job="node-exporter"} == 0
                    for: 5m
                    labels:
                      severity: critical
                    annotations:
                      summary: "Node {{ $labels.instance }} is down"
                      description: "Node exporter on {{ $labels.instance }} has been down for more than 5 minutes"

                  - alert: PodCrashLooping
                    expr: rate(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[15m]) > 0
                    for: 5m
                    labels:
                      severity: warning
                    annotations:
                      summary: "Pod {{ $labels.namespace }}/{{ $labels.pod }} is crash looping"
                      description: "Pod {{ $labels.namespace }}/{{ $labels.pod }} has restarted {{ $value }} times in the last 15 minutes"

                  - alert: PersistentVolumeFillingUp
                    expr: kubelet_volume_stats_available_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes < 0.1
                    for: 10m
                    labels:
                      severity: warning
                    annotations:
                      summary: "PV {{ $labels.persistentvolumeclaim }} filling up"
                      description: "PV {{ $labels.persistentvolumeclaim }} in {{ $labels.namespace }} has less than 10% space available"
        dest: /tmp/infrastructure-alerts.yaml
        mode: '0644'

    - name: Apply infrastructure alerts
      command: kubectl apply -f /tmp/infrastructure-alerts.yaml
      register: alerts_apply
      changed_when: "'created' in alerts_apply.stdout or 'configured' in alerts_apply.stdout"

    - name: Remove temporary alerts file
      file:
        path: /tmp/infrastructure-alerts.yaml
        state: absent
  when: alerting_rules_enabled
  tags: [monitoring, alerts]

- name: Monitoring configuration summary
  debug:
    msg:
      - "========================================="
      - "Monitoring Stack Verification Complete"
      - "========================================="
      - "✓ Prometheus: {{ prometheus_ready.rc == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "✓ Grafana: {{ grafana_ready.rc == 0 | ternary('Ready', 'Pending') }}"
      - "✓ Alertmanager: {{ alertmanager_pods.rc == 0 | ternary('Running', 'Pending') }}"
      - "✓ ServiceMonitors: {{ servicemonitor_count }}"
      - "✓ Active targets: {{ active_targets | default('N/A') }}"
      - "✓ Alerting rules: {{ alerting_rules_enabled | ternary('Configured', 'Disabled') }}"
      - "========================================="
  tags: [monitoring]
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/monitoring/templates/dashboard-import-job.yaml.j2">
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: grafana-dashboard-import
  namespace: {{ monitoring_namespace }}
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: dashboard-importer
          image: curlimages/curl:8.11.1
          command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              set -e
              GRAFANA_URL="http://{{ grafana_service_name }}.{{ monitoring_namespace }}.svc.cluster.local:{{ grafana_port }}"

              echo "Importing Grafana dashboards..."
              {% for dashboard in grafana_dashboards %}
              echo "Importing {{ dashboard.name }} (ID: {{ dashboard.id }})"
              curl -X POST "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/dashboards/import" \
                -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
                -u "{{ grafana_admin_user }}:${GRAFANA_PASSWORD}" \
                -d "{\"dashboard\":{\"id\":{{ dashboard.id }}},\"overwrite\":true,\"inputs\":[]}" \
                || echo "Failed to import {{ dashboard.name }}"
              {% endfor %}

              echo "Dashboard import complete"
          env:
            - name: GRAFANA_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{ grafana_service_name }}
                  key: admin-password
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/monitoring/README.md">
# Monitoring Role

Verifies and configures the monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki).

## Overview

This role:
- Verifies Prometheus is scraping targets
- Checks Grafana dashboards are loaded
- Validates Loki log collection
- Configures alerting rules

## Requirements

- Kubernetes cluster running
- Monitoring stack deployed via Helm
- kubectl configured on control node

## Role Variables

See `defaults/main.yml` for full list:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `prometheus_namespace` | Prometheus namespace |
| `grafana_namespace` | Grafana namespace |
| `alertmanager_enabled` | Enable Alertmanager configuration |
| `slack_webhook_url` | Slack webhook for alerts |

## Components

Deployed via the monitoring Helm chart:
- **Prometheus** - Metrics collection (30s scrape interval)
- **Grafana** - Dashboards and visualization
- **Loki** - Log aggregation
- **Promtail** - Log collection
- **Alertmanager** - Alert routing

## Dependencies

- `infrastructure` role (deploys monitoring)
- Vault (for credentials)

## Example Playbook

```yaml
- hosts: k8s_masters
  roles:
    - monitoring
  tags: [monitoring]
```

## Tags

- `monitoring` - Run all monitoring tasks
- `monitoring, verify` - Verify monitoring stack
- `monitoring, alerts` - Configure alerting

## Alerting

Default alert rules:
- NodeDown (critical): Node offline > 5min
- PodCrashLooping (warning): Restarts > 0 in 15min
- PVFillingUp (warning): PV < 10% free

## Dashboards

Pre-configured Grafana dashboards:
- Kubernetes cluster overview
- Node Exporter metrics
- Traefik ingress
- PostgreSQL database
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin/templates/pangolin-server.service.j2">
[Unit]
Description=Pangolin WireGuard VPN Server
Documentation=https://github.com/lvlcn-t/pangolin
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=root
Group=root

# Working directory
WorkingDirectory={{ pangolin_install_dir }}

# Environment
Environment="PANGOLIN_CONFIG={{ pangolin_config_dir }}/config.yaml"
Environment="PANGOLIN_LOG_LEVEL=info"

# Executable
ExecStart={{ pangolin_install_dir }}/pangolin server \
    --config {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/config.yaml \
    --log-level info \
    --log-format json

# Restart policy
Restart=always
RestartSec=10s

# Security hardening
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths={{ pangolin_config_dir }} /var/log/pangolin
ProtectKernelTunables=true
ProtectKernelModules=false
ProtectControlGroups=true
RestrictRealtime=true
RestrictNamespaces=false
LockPersonality=true
MemoryDenyWriteExecute=false
RestrictAddressFamilies=AF_UNIX AF_INET AF_INET6 AF_NETLINK
SystemCallFilter=@system-service
SystemCallErrorNumber=EPERM
AmbientCapabilities=CAP_NET_ADMIN CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE CAP_NET_RAW

# Limits
LimitNOFILE=65536
LimitNPROC=4096

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin/templates/wg0-server.conf.j2">
# WireGuard VPN Server Configuration
# Generated by Ansible
# Gateway: {{ inventory_hostname }}

[Interface]
PrivateKey = {{ wireguard_server_private_key }}
Address = {{ wireguard_server_ip }}/24
ListenPort = {{ wireguard_port }}
PostUp = iptables -A FORWARD -i %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o {{ ansible_default_ipv4.interface }} -j MASQUERADE
PostDown = iptables -D FORWARD -i %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -o {{ ansible_default_ipv4.interface }} -j MASQUERADE

{% if groups['masters'] is defined %}
# K8s master nodes (10.99.0.10-19)
{% for host in groups['masters'] %}
[Peer]
# {{ host }}
PublicKey = {{ vpn.pangolin.client_keys[host].public_key }}
AllowedIPs = {{ pangolin_node_ip_prefix }}.{{ 10 + loop.index0 }}/32
{% endfor %}
{% endif %}

{% if groups['workers'] is defined %}
# K8s worker nodes (10.99.0.20-29)
{% for host in groups['workers'] %}
[Peer]
# {{ host }}
PublicKey = {{ vpn.pangolin.client_keys[host].public_key }}
AllowedIPs = {{ pangolin_node_ip_prefix }}.{{ 20 + loop.index0 }}/32
{% endfor %}
{% endif %}
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin/templates/wg0.conf.j2">
# WireGuard VPN Client Configuration
# Generated by Ansible
# Node: {{ inventory_hostname }}

[Interface]
PrivateKey = {{ wireguard_client_private_key }}
Address = {{ node_wireguard_ip }}/24

[Peer]
PublicKey = {{ wireguard_gateway_public_key }}
AllowedIPs = {{ wireguard_network }}
Endpoint = {{ pangolin_gateway_endpoint }}
PersistentKeepalive = 25
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin/README.md">
# Pangolin VPN Role

Ansible role for deploying Pangolin WireGuard VPN infrastructure with dual-mode support: gateway (VPS server) and node (cluster clients).

## Overview

This role automates the deployment of a WireGuard-based VPN tunnel using:
- **Pangolin Server** - WireGuard VPN server (gateway mode)
- **Gerbil** - WireGuard interface orchestrator (gateway mode)
- **Traefik** - Edge router for HTTP/HTTPS traffic (gateway mode)
- **Newt** - WireGuard VPN client (node mode)

## Architecture

```
Internet → Gateway VPS (Pangolin + Traefik) → VPN Tunnel → K8s Cluster Nodes (Newt)
                                                  ↓
                                           WireGuard Network
                                            10.99.0.0/24
```

## Requirements

- Ansible 2.20.1 or higher
- Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 or Debian 11/12
- Root access on target hosts
- WireGuard kernel module support

## Role Variables

### Required Variables (in `group_vars/all/vault.yml`)

```yaml
vault:
  vpn:
    pangolin:
      server_private_key: 'WireGuard private key'
      server_public_key: 'WireGuard public key'
      preshared_key: 'WireGuard preshared key'
      client_keys:
        hostname:
          private_key: 'Client WireGuard private key'
          public_key: 'Client WireGuard public key'
```

### Defaults (in `defaults/main.yml`)

```yaml
# Mode: 'gateway' or 'node'
pangolin_mode: "node"

# Versions
pangolin_version: "1.15.0"
gerbil_version: "1.3.0"
traefik_version: "v3.4.0"
newt_version: "1.2.0"

# Network
wireguard_network: "10.99.0.0/24"
wireguard_server_ip: "10.99.0.1"
wireguard_port: 51820
```

## Dependencies

None.

## Example Playbook

### Gateway Deployment (VPS)

```yaml
- hosts: gateway
  roles:
    - role: pangolin
      vars:
        pangolin_mode: gateway
```

### Node Deployment (K8s Cluster)

```yaml
- hosts: k8s_masters:k8s_workers
  roles:
    - role: pangolin
      vars:
        pangolin_mode: node
```

## Inventory Configuration

### gateway.ini

```ini
[gateway]
vps-gateway ansible_host=80.90.178.207 ansible_user=root

[gateway:vars]
pangolin_mode=gateway
```

### master.ini

```ini
[k8s_masters]
master-1 ansible_host=192.168.100.10 ansible_user=root

[k8s_masters:vars]
pangolin_mode=node
```

### node.ini

```ini
[k8s_workers]
worker-1 ansible_host=192.168.100.11 ansible_user=root
worker-2 ansible_host=192.168.100.12 ansible_user=root

[k8s_workers:vars]
pangolin_mode=node
```

## Tags

- `pangolin` - All tasks
- `common` - Common setup tasks
- `gateway` - Gateway-specific tasks
- `node` - Node-specific tasks
- `packages` - Package installation
- `sysctl` - Kernel parameters
- `firewall` - UFW configuration
- `directories` - Directory creation
- `download` - Binary downloads
- `install` - Binary installation
- `config` - Configuration deployment
- `systemd` - Systemd unit management
- `service` - Service management
- `iptables` - IPtables NAT rules (gateway only)
- `verify` - Deployment verification

## Usage Examples

### Deploy Gateway Only

```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory/gateway.ini all.yml --tags pangolin,gateway
```

### Deploy Nodes Only

```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory/master.ini,inventory/node.ini all.yml --tags pangolin,node
```

### Deploy All

```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.ini all.yml --tags pangolin
```

### Verify Tunnel Connectivity

From a node:
```bash
ping 10.99.0.1  # Gateway server IP
```

From gateway:
```bash
systemctl status pangolin gerbil traefik
wg show
```

From node:
```bash
systemctl status newt
wg show
```

## WireGuard Key Generation

Generate keys for Vault:

```bash
# Server keys
wg genkey | tee server.key | wg pubkey > server.pub

# Client keys (per host)
wg genkey | tee client-host1.key | wg pubkey > client-host1.pub

# Preshared key (shared across all peers)
wg genpsk > preshared.key
```

## IP Address Allocation

The role automatically assigns WireGuard IPs:
- Gateway: `10.99.0.1`
- K8s Masters: `10.99.0.10-19` (based on inventory position)
- K8s Workers: `10.99.0.20-29` (based on inventory position)

## Firewall Rules

### Gateway (VPS)
- TCP: 22 (SSH), 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS)
- UDP: 51820 (WireGuard)

### Nodes (Cluster)
- TCP: 22 (SSH)
- UDP: 51820 (WireGuard)

## Files and Templates

### Tasks
- `main.yml` - Task router
- `common.yml` - Common setup for gateway/nodes
- `gateway.yml` - Gateway deployment
- `node.yml` - Node deployment

### Templates (Gateway)
- `pangolin-config.yaml.j2` - Pangolin server config
- `traefik-gateway.yml.j2` - Traefik edge router config
- `pangolin-server.service.j2` - Systemd unit
- `gerbil.service.j2` - Systemd unit
- `traefik.service.j2` - Systemd unit

### Templates (Node)
- `newt-config.yaml.j2` - Newt client config
- `newt.service.j2` - Systemd unit

## Troubleshooting

### Tunnel Not Connecting

Check systemd service status:
```bash
systemctl status pangolin  # Gateway
systemctl status newt      # Node
```

Check WireGuard interface:
```bash
wg show
ip addr show wg0
```

Check logs:
```bash
journalctl -u pangolin -f  # Gateway
journalctl -u newt -f      # Node
```

### Firewall Issues

Check UFW status:
```bash
ufw status verbose
```

Check iptables NAT (gateway):
```bash
iptables -t nat -L -n -v
```

### DNS Resolution Issues

Check resolv.conf:
```bash
cat /etc/resolv.conf
```

Test DNS:
```bash
nslookup kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local
```

## License

MIT

## Author

Lvov Valery
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin_vps/defaults/main.yml">
---
# Pangolin VPS Docker Deployment - Default Variables
# These can be overridden in group_vars/all/vars.yml or playbook vars

# Version pinning - always use specific versions, never latest
pangolin_version: "1.3.1"
gerbil_version: "1.1.0"
traefik_version: "3.2"

# Installation paths
pangolin_base_dir: "/opt/pangolin"
pangolin_config_dir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/config"
pangolin_data_dir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/data"
traefik_config_dir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/traefik"
letsencrypt_dir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/letsencrypt"

# WireGuard network configuration
wireguard_network: "10.99.0.0/24"
wireguard_server_ip: "10.99.0.1"
wireguard_port: 51820
wireguard_interface: "wg0"

# Gateway endpoints (derived from inventory)
pangolin_gateway_public_ip: "{{ hostvars[groups['gateway'][0]]['ansible_host'] }}"
pangolin_gateway_endpoint: "{{ pangolin_gateway_public_ip }}:{{ wireguard_port }}"

# Traefik ports
traefik_http_port: 80
traefik_https_port: 443
traefik_admin_port: 3001

# Cluster connection (NodePorts on cluster Traefik)
cluster_nodeport_http: 30080
cluster_nodeport_https: 30443

# Node IP allocation
pangolin_node_ip_prefix: "10.99.0"

# Docker configuration
docker_network_name: "pangolin"
docker_restart_policy: "unless-stopped"

# Health check configuration
healthcheck_interval: "30s"
healthcheck_timeout: "10s"
healthcheck_retries: 3
healthcheck_start_period: "10s"

# Logging configuration
log_level: "info"
log_max_size: "10m"
log_max_file: 3

# System tuning
sysctl_config:
  net.ipv4.ip_forward: 1
  net.ipv4.conf.all.src_valid_mark: 1
  net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding: 1
  net.core.rmem_max: 2500000
  net.core.wmem_max: 2500000

# Firewall ports
firewall_allowed_tcp_ports:
  - "{{ ssh.port | default(22) }}"
  - "{{ traefik_http_port }}"
  - "{{ traefik_https_port }}"
  - "{{ traefik_admin_port }}"
firewall_allowed_udp_ports:
  - "{{ wireguard_port }}"
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin_vps/handlers/main.yml">
---
# Pangolin VPS Docker Handlers

- name: restart pangolin-docker
  command: docker compose restart
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  listen: restart pangolin-docker

- name: stop pangolin-docker
  command: docker compose down
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  listen: stop pangolin-docker

- name: start pangolin-docker
  command: docker compose up -d
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  listen: start pangolin-docker

- name: reload systemd
  systemd:
    daemon_reload: yes
  listen: reload systemd

- name: reload ufw
  ufw:
    state: reloaded
  listen: reload ufw
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin_vps/meta/main.yml">
---
# Role metadata for pangolin_vps

galaxy_info:
  role_name: pangolin_vps
  author: Self-Hosted Infrastructure
  description: Deploy Pangolin VPN Server via Docker Compose on VPS gateway
  company: Self-Hosted
  license: MIT
  min_ansible_version: "2.14"
  platforms:
    - name: Ubuntu
      versions:
        - all
    - name: Debian
      versions:
        - all
  galaxy_tags:
    - vpn
    - wireguard
    - pangolin
    - docker
    - gateway
    - traefik

dependencies:
  - role: docker
    vars:
      docker_edition: ce
</file>

<file path="ansible/roles/pangolin_vps/tasks/main.yml">
---
# Pangolin VPS Docker Deployment
# Deploys Pangolin Server stack on VPS via Docker Compose
#
# Architecture:
#   Internet -> VPS (Docker: Pangolin + Gerbil + Traefik)
#                 |
#                 | WireGuard/Newt tunnel
#                 v
#            K8s Cluster: Newt Client Pod -> Cluster Traefik -> Services

- name: Display deployment info
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Deploying Pangolin VPN Server via Docker"
      - "VPS IP: {{ pangolin_gateway_public_ip }}"
      - "Domain: {{ domain.public }}"
      - "WireGuard Port: {{ wireguard_port }}"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, always]

# =============================================================================
# PREREQUISITES
# =============================================================================

- name: Ensure Docker is installed
  command: docker --version
  register: docker_check
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: docker_check.rc != 0
  tags: [pangolin-vps, prereq]

- name: Ensure Docker Compose plugin is available
  command: docker compose version
  register: compose_check
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: compose_check.rc != 0
  tags: [pangolin-vps, prereq]

- name: Install required packages
  apt:
    name:
      - wireguard-tools
      - iptables
      - curl
    state: present
    update_cache: yes
  tags: [pangolin-vps, prereq]

# =============================================================================
# SYSTEM CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

- name: Configure sysctl for VPN
  sysctl:
    name: "{{ item.key }}"
    value: "{{ item.value }}"
    state: present
    reload: yes
    sysctl_file: /etc/sysctl.d/99-pangolin.conf
  loop: "{{ sysctl_config | dict2items }}"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, sysctl]

# =============================================================================
# DIRECTORY STRUCTURE
# =============================================================================

- name: Create Pangolin directory structure
  file:
    path: "{{ item }}"
    state: directory
    mode: '0755'
    owner: root
    group: root
  loop:
    - "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
    - "{{ pangolin_config_dir }}"
    - "{{ pangolin_data_dir }}"
    - "{{ traefik_config_dir }}"
    - "{{ traefik_config_dir }}/dynamic"
    - "{{ letsencrypt_dir }}"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, dirs]

# =============================================================================
# SECRETS HANDLING
# =============================================================================

- name: Check if WireGuard keys exist
  stat:
    path: "{{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key"
  register: wg_key_file
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

- name: Generate WireGuard keys if not exist
  shell: |
    wg genkey > {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key
    wg pubkey < {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key > {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_public.key
    chmod 600 {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key
    chmod 644 {{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_public.key
  args:
    creates: "{{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key"
  when: not wg_key_file.stat.exists
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

- name: Read WireGuard private key
  slurp:
    src: "{{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_private.key"
  register: wg_private_key_file
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

- name: Read WireGuard public key
  slurp:
    src: "{{ pangolin_config_dir }}/wg_public.key"
  register: wg_public_key_file
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

- name: Set WireGuard keys
  set_fact:
    wireguard_server_private_key: "{{ wg_private_key_file.content | b64decode | trim }}"
    wireguard_server_public_key: "{{ wg_public_key_file.content | b64decode | trim }}"
  no_log: true
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

- name: Generate Pangolin secret
  set_fact:
    pangolin_secret: "{{ lookup('password', '/dev/null chars=ascii_letters,digits length=64') }}"
  no_log: true
  tags: [pangolin-vps, secrets]

# =============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION FILES
# =============================================================================

- name: Deploy Pangolin configuration
  template:
    src: config.yml.j2
    dest: "{{ pangolin_data_dir }}/config.yml"
    mode: '0600'
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart pangolin-docker
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

- name: Deploy Traefik static configuration
  template:
    src: traefik.yml.j2
    dest: "{{ traefik_config_dir }}/traefik.yml"
    mode: '0644'
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart pangolin-docker
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

- name: Deploy Traefik dynamic configuration
  template:
    src: dynamic.yml.j2
    dest: "{{ traefik_config_dir }}/dynamic/cluster.yml"
    mode: '0644'
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart pangolin-docker
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

- name: Create ACME storage file with correct permissions
  file:
    path: "{{ letsencrypt_dir }}/acme.json"
    state: touch
    mode: '0600'
    owner: root
    group: root
  changed_when: false
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

- name: Deploy Docker Compose file
  template:
    src: docker-compose.yml.j2
    dest: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/docker-compose.yml"
    mode: '0644'
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart pangolin-docker
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

- name: Deploy environment file
  template:
    src: env.j2
    dest: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}/.env"
    mode: '0600'
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart pangolin-docker
  tags: [pangolin-vps, config]

# =============================================================================
# FIREWALL
# =============================================================================

- name: Configure UFW - allow SSH
  ufw:
    rule: allow
    port: "{{ ssh.port | default(22) }}"
    proto: tcp
  when: ansible_os_family | default('Debian') == "Debian"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, firewall]

- name: Configure UFW - allow HTTP
  ufw:
    rule: allow
    port: "{{ traefik_http_port }}"
    proto: tcp
  when: ansible_os_family | default('Debian') == "Debian"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, firewall]

- name: Configure UFW - allow HTTPS
  ufw:
    rule: allow
    port: "{{ traefik_https_port }}"
    proto: tcp
  when: ansible_os_family | default('Debian') == "Debian"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, firewall]

- name: Configure UFW - allow WireGuard
  ufw:
    rule: allow
    port: "{{ wireguard_port }}"
    proto: udp
  when: ansible_os_family | default('Debian') == "Debian"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, firewall]

- name: Enable UFW
  ufw:
    state: enabled
    policy: deny
    direction: incoming
  when: ansible_os_family | default('Debian') == "Debian"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, firewall]

# =============================================================================
# DOCKER NETWORK
# =============================================================================

- name: Create Docker network for Pangolin
  docker_network:
    name: "{{ docker_network_name }}"
    state: present
  tags: [pangolin-vps, docker]

# =============================================================================
# DEPLOYMENT
# =============================================================================

- name: Pull Docker images
  command: docker compose pull
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  register: pull_result
  changed_when: "'Pulling' in pull_result.stdout or 'Downloaded' in pull_result.stdout"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, deploy]

- name: Start Pangolin stack
  command: docker compose up -d
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  register: compose_up
  changed_when: "'Creating' in compose_up.stdout or 'Starting' in compose_up.stdout or 'Recreating' in compose_up.stdout"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, deploy]

# =============================================================================
# SYSTEMD SERVICE
# =============================================================================

- name: Deploy systemd service for Pangolin stack
  template:
    src: pangolin-docker.service.j2
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/pangolin-docker.service
    mode: '0644'
  notify: reload systemd
  tags: [pangolin-vps, systemd]

- name: Enable Pangolin Docker service
  systemd:
    name: pangolin-docker
    enabled: yes
    daemon_reload: yes
  tags: [pangolin-vps, systemd]

# =============================================================================
# VERIFICATION
# =============================================================================

- name: Wait for Traefik to be healthy
  uri:
    url: "http://localhost:{{ traefik_http_port }}/ping"
    status_code: 200
    timeout: 5
  register: traefik_health
  retries: 30
  delay: 5
  until: traefik_health.status == 200
  ignore_errors: yes
  tags: [pangolin-vps, verify]

- name: Check Docker containers status
  command: docker compose ps
  args:
    chdir: "{{ pangolin_base_dir }}"
  register: compose_status
  changed_when: false
  tags: [pangolin-vps, verify]

- name: Display container status
  debug:
    msg: "{{ compose_status.stdout_lines }}"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, verify]

- name: Display deployment summary
  debug:
    msg:
      - "Pangolin VPS deployment completed"
      - "Admin UI: https://pangolin.{{ domain.public }}"
      - "WireGuard Endpoint: {{ pangolin_gateway_endpoint }}"
      - "Traefik Health: {{ 'OK' if (traefik_health.status | default(0)) == 200 else 'PENDING' }}"
  tags: [pangolin-vps, always]
</file>

# Pangolin Server Configuration
# Generated by Ansible
# Documentation: https://github.com/fosrl/pangolin

app:
  dashboard_url: "https://pangolin.{{ domain.public }}"
  log_level: "{{ log_level }}"

domains:
  domain1:
    base_domain: "{{ domain.public }}"
    cert_resolver: "letsencrypt"
    prefer_wildcard_cert: true

server:
  external_port: 3000
  internal_port: 3001
  next_port: 3002
  internal_hostname: "pangolin"
  secret: "{{ pangolin_secret }}"
  session_cookie_name: "p_session"
  resource_access_token_param: "p_token"
  resource_access_token_headers:
    id: "P-Access-Token-Id"
    token: "P-Access-Token"

# WireGuard tunnel configuration
gerbil:
  start_port: 51820
  base_endpoint: "{{ pangolin_gateway_public_ip }}"
  use_subdomain: false
  block_size: 24
  site_block_size: 30
  subnet_group: "{{ wireguard_network | regex_replace('/.*', '') }}"

# Rate limiting
rate_limit:
  window_size_seconds: 60
  max_requests: 100

# Users configuration
users:
  server_admin:
    email: "{{ tls.letsencrypt.email }}"
    password: "${ADMIN_PASSWORD}"

# Email configuration (optional)
email:
  enabled: false
{% if smtp is defined and smtp.host is defined %}
  smtp_host: "{{ smtp.host }}"
  smtp_port: {{ smtp.port | default(587) }}
  smtp_user: "{{ smtp.username | default('') }}"
  smtp_password: "${SMTP_PASSWORD}"
  smtp_from: "{{ smtp.from_email | default('noreply@' + domain.public) }}"
{% endif %}

# Traefik integration
traefik:
  user: "admin"
  password: "${TRAEFIK_PASSWORD}"
  cert_resolver: "letsencrypt"
  http_entrypoint: "web"
  https_entrypoint: "websecure"

# Security settings
